It '''reopened on November 3, 2021'''. == Related Topics == {{Commonscat|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture}} * {{ill|Category:People from Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|Category:奈良県立橿原考古学研究所の人物}} == External Links == * {{official|http://www.kashikoken.jp/}} * {{Facebook|kashikoken|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute}} {{coord|region:JP|display=title}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute}} [[Category:Museums in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Archaeological museums in Japan]] [[Category:Research institutes in Japan]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan (archaeological materials)]] [[Category:Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:Buildings and structures completed in 1980]] [[Category:Buildings and structures completed in 1990]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kashihara Archaeological Institute-qq]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:Https://de.wikipedia.org/wiki/]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Kashihara Archaeological Institute]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]] [[de:Arch%C3%A4ologisches Forschungsinstitut der Pr%C3%A4fektur Nara in Kashihara]] [[ja:%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E7%9C%8C%E7%AB%8B%E6%A9%BF%E5%8E%9F%E8%80%83%E5%8F%A4%E5%AD%A6%E7%A0%94%E7%A9%B6%E6%89%80]]

[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kashihara Archaeological Institute-qq]] {{pp-vandalism|small=yes}} [[file:Kashihara Archaeological Institute , Nara prefecture.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute]] The '''Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute''' (奈良県立橿原考古学研究所, *Nara Kenritsu Kashihara Kōkogaku Kenkyūjo*) is a '''prefectural institution''' located in {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市}}, specializing in the research and investigation of '''buried cultural properties''' (埋蔵文化財). It is an institution under the jurisdiction of '''Nara Prefecture'''. As one of the oldest '''public institutions for archaeological research''' still in operation today, it is sometimes abbreviated as '''Kashikōken''' (橿考研). The institute has conducted '''excavations of major historical sites''' within Nara Prefecture, including the '''Makimuku Ruins''' ({{ill|Makimuku ruins|ja|纒向遺跡}}), {{ill|Hokenoyama Kofun|ja|ホケノ山古墳}}, {{ill|Kurozuka Kofun|ja|黒塚古墳}}, {{ill|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun|ja|桜井茶臼山古墳}}, {{ill|Mesuriyama Kofun|ja|メスリ山古墳}}, {{ill|Fujinoki Kofun|ja|藤ノ木古墳}}, and {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}}. It has gained '''national recognition''' for its work on '''excavations of ancient capitals''' such as '''Asuka-kyō''' ({{ill|Asuka-dera|ja|飛鳥京跡}}), '''Tōdai-ji''' ({{ill|Tōdai-ji|ja|東大寺}}), and '''Tōshōdai-ji''' ({{ill|Tōshōdai-ji|ja|唐招提寺}}). The institute also manages an '''affiliated museum''' and operates a '''cultural property storage and research center''' in {{ill|Uda, Nara|ja|宇陀市}}. == History == * '''1938 (Shōwa 13)''' - Archaeological surveys of '''Kashihara Shrine’s outer precincts''' were conducted as part of the '''2600th Anniversary of Japan’s Founding Project''' (紀元二千六百年記念行事). '''Masao Suenaga''' ({{ill|Masao Suenaga|ja|末永雅雄}}) from '''Kyoto University''' led the survey on '''September 13''', which later became the institute’s '''founding date'''. * '''1951 (Shōwa 26)''' - The '''Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute''' was officially established with '''Masao Suenaga''' as the first director. * '''1972 (Shōwa 47)''' - The '''Takamatsuzuka Kofun murals''' were discovered during an excavation by the institute. * '''1974 (Shōwa 49)''' - The former '''Kashihara Kōen Archaeological Museum''' became an '''affiliated facility''', later renamed '''Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum'''. * '''1985 (Shōwa 60)''' - First excavation of '''Fujinoki Kofun'''. * '''1990 (Heisei 2)''' - The institute’s '''new headquarters''' was completed. * '''1997 (Heisei 9)''' - Excavation of '''Kurozuka Kofun'''. * '''2014 (Heisei 26)''' - '''Cultural Property Storage and Research Center''' established in the former '''Nara Prefectural Murō High School'''. * '''2015 (Heisei 27)''' - The institute was transferred from the '''Nara Prefectural Board of Education''' to the '''Governor’s Office of Nara Prefecture'''. == Organization == * The '''Director-General''' oversees the '''Deputy Director''' and '''Museum Director'''. * The '''Deputy Director''' supervises: * '''Planning Division''' (企画部) * '''Planning Section''' (企画課) * '''Documentation Section''' (資料課) * '''Excavation Division''' (調査部) * '''Excavation Section''' (調査課) * '''General Affairs Section''' (総務課) * The '''Museum Director''' supervises the '''Deputy Museum Director''' and the '''Curatorial Section''' (学芸課). == Affiliated Museum == {{博物館 |名称 = Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum |画像 = Kashiharakouen-koukogaku02.jpg|Affiliated Museum |imagesize = 250 |正式名称 = 奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館 |専門分野 = {{ill|Archaeology|ja|考古学}}・{{ill|Buried Cultural Properties|ja|埋蔵文化財}} |所在地 = [[Nara Prefecture]] [[Kashihara, Nara]] |アクセス = [[Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu]] {{ill|Kintetsu Kashihara Line|ja|近鉄橿原線}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Yoshino Line|ja|近鉄吉野線}} - [[Kashiharajingū-mae Station]] (15-min walk)
[[Kintetsu Kashihara Line]] - [[Unebigoryōmae Station]] (5-min walk) |URL = [http://www.kashikoken.jp/museum/ Official Website] }} The '''museum houses approximately 3,700 artifacts''' spanning from the '''Paleolithic period to the Muromachi period'''.{{Cite web|和書|title=Nara's Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum Reopens|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211102110118/https://nordot.app/828174813890314240|website=Kyodo News|accessdate=2021-11-03|language=ja}} The museum was '''closed for renovations''' from '''December 24, 2018''', due to updates to the '''air conditioning system''' and concerns over the presence of '''harmful substances inside display cases'''. It '''reopened on November 3, 2021'''. == Related Topics == {{Commonscat|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture}} * {{ill|Category:People from Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|Category:奈良県立橿原考古学研究所の人物}} == External Links == * {{official|http://www.kashikoken.jp/}} * {{Facebook|kashikoken|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute}} {{coord|region:JP|display=title}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute}} [[Category:Museums in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Archaeological museums in Japan]] [[Category:Research institutes in Japan]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan (archaeological materials)]] [[Category:Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:Buildings and structures completed in 1980]] [[Category:Buildings and structures completed in 1990]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kashihara Archaeological Institute-qq]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:Https://de.wikipedia.org/wiki/]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Kashihara Archaeological Institute]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]] [[de:Arch%C3%A4ologisches Forschungsinstitut der Pr%C3%A4fektur Nara in Kashihara]] [[ja:%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E7%9C%8C%E7%AB%8B%E6%A9%BF%E5%8E%9F%E8%80%83%E5%8F%A4%E5%AD%A6%E7%A0%94%E7%A9%B6%E6%89%80]]

The institute was transferred from the '''Nara Prefectural Board of Education''' to the '''Governor’s Office of Nara Prefecture'''. == Organization == * The '''Director-General''' oversees the '''Deputy Director''' and '''Museum Director'''. * The '''Deputy Director''' supervises: * '''Planning Division''' (企画部) * '''Planning Section''' (企画課) * '''Documentation Section''' (資料課) * '''Excavation Division''' (調査部) * '''Excavation Section''' (調査課) * '''General Affairs Section''' (総務課) * The '''Museum Director''' supervises the '''Deputy Museum Director''' and the '''Curatorial Section''' (学芸課). == Affiliated Museum == {{博物館 |名称 = Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum |画像 = Kashiharakouen-koukogaku02.jpg|Affiliated Museum |imagesize = 250 |正式名称 = 奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館 |専門分野 = {{ill|Archaeology|ja|考古学}}・{{ill|Buried Cultural Properties|ja|埋蔵文化財}} |所在地 = [[Nara Prefecture]] [[Kashihara, Nara]] |アクセス = [[Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu]] {{ill|Kintetsu Kashihara Line|ja|近鉄橿原線}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Yoshino Line|ja|近鉄吉野線}} - [[Kashiharajingū-mae Station]] (15-min walk)
[[Kintetsu Kashihara Line]] - [[Unebigoryōmae Station]] (5-min walk) |URL = [http://www.kashikoken.jp/museum/ Official Website] }} The '''museum houses approximately 3,700 artifacts''' spanning from the '''Paleolithic period to the Muromachi period'''.{{Cite web|和書|title=Nara's Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum Reopens|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211102110118/https://nordot.app/828174813890314240|website=Kyodo News|accessdate=2021-11-03|language=ja}} The museum was '''closed for renovations''' from '''December 24, 2018''', due to updates to the '''air conditioning system''' and concerns over the presence of '''harmful substances inside display cases'''. [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kashihara Archaeological Institute-qq]] {{pp-vandalism|small=yes}} [[file:Kashihara Archaeological Institute , Nara prefecture.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute]] The '''Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute''' (奈良県立橿原考古学研究所, *Nara Kenritsu Kashihara Kōkogaku Kenkyūjo*) is a '''prefectural institution''' located in {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市}}, specializing in the research and investigation of '''buried cultural properties''' (埋蔵文化財). It is an institution under the jurisdiction of '''Nara Prefecture'''. As one of the oldest '''public institutions for archaeological research''' still in operation today, it is sometimes abbreviated as '''Kashikōken''' (橿考研). The institute has conducted '''excavations of major historical sites''' within Nara Prefecture, including the '''Makimuku Ruins''' ({{ill|Makimuku ruins|ja|纒向遺跡}}), {{ill|Hokenoyama Kofun|ja|ホケノ山古墳}}, {{ill|Kurozuka Kofun|ja|黒塚古墳}}, {{ill|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun|ja|桜井茶臼山古墳}}, {{ill|Mesuriyama Kofun|ja|メスリ山古墳}}, {{ill|Fujinoki Kofun|ja|藤ノ木古墳}}, and {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}}. It has gained '''national recognition''' for its work on '''excavations of ancient capitals''' such as '''Asuka-kyō''' ({{ill|Asuka-dera|ja|飛鳥京跡}}), '''Tōdai-ji''' ({{ill|Tōdai-ji|ja|東大寺}}), and '''Tōshōdai-ji''' ({{ill|Tōshōdai-ji|ja|唐招提寺}}). The institute also manages an '''affiliated museum''' and operates a '''cultural property storage and research center''' in {{ill|Uda, Nara|ja|宇陀市}}. == History == * '''1938 (Shōwa 13)''' - Archaeological surveys of '''Kashihara Shrine’s outer precincts''' were conducted as part of the '''2600th Anniversary of Japan’s Founding Project''' (紀元二千六百年記念行事). '''Masao Suenaga''' ({{ill|Masao Suenaga|ja|末永雅雄}}) from '''Kyoto University''' led the survey on '''September 13''', which later became the institute’s '''founding date'''. * '''1951 (Shōwa 26)''' - The '''Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute''' was officially established with '''Masao Suenaga''' as the first director. * '''1972 (Shōwa 47)''' - The '''Takamatsuzuka Kofun murals''' were discovered during an excavation by the institute. * '''1974 (Shōwa 49)''' - The former '''Kashihara Kōen Archaeological Museum''' became an '''affiliated facility''', later renamed '''Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum'''. * '''1985 (Shōwa 60)''' - First excavation of '''Fujinoki Kofun'''. * '''1990 (Heisei 2)''' - The institute’s '''new headquarters''' was completed. * '''1997 (Heisei 9)''' - Excavation of '''Kurozuka Kofun'''. * '''2014 (Heisei 26)''' - '''Cultural Property Storage and Research Center''' established in the former '''Nara Prefectural Murō High School'''. * '''2015 (Heisei 27)''' - The institute was transferred from the '''Nara Prefectural Board of Education''' to the '''Governor’s Office of Nara Prefecture'''. == Organization == * The '''Director-General''' oversees the '''Deputy Director''' and '''Museum Director'''. * The '''Deputy Director''' supervises: * '''Planning Division''' (企画部) * '''Planning Section''' (企画課) * '''Documentation Section''' (資料課) * '''Excavation Division''' (調査部) * '''Excavation Section''' (調査課) * '''General Affairs Section''' (総務課) * The '''Museum Director''' supervises the '''Deputy Museum Director''' and the '''Curatorial Section''' (学芸課). == Affiliated Museum == {{博物館 |名称 = Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum |画像 = Kashiharakouen-koukogaku02.jpg|Affiliated Museum |imagesize = 250 |正式名称 = 奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館 |専門分野 = {{ill|Archaeology|ja|考古学}}・{{ill|Buried Cultural Properties|ja|埋蔵文化財}} |所在地 = [[Nara Prefecture]] [[Kashihara, Nara]] |アクセス = [[Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu]] {{ill|Kintetsu Kashihara Line|ja|近鉄橿原線}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Yoshino Line|ja|近鉄吉野線}} - [[Kashiharajingū-mae Station]] (15-min walk)
[[Kintetsu Kashihara Line]] - [[Unebigoryōmae Station]] (5-min walk) |URL = [http://www.kashikoken.jp/museum/ Official Website] }} The '''museum houses approximately 3,700 artifacts''' spanning from the '''Paleolithic period to the Muromachi period'''.{{Cite web|和書|title=Nara's Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum Reopens|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211102110118/https://nordot.app/828174813890314240|website=Kyodo News|accessdate=2021-11-03|language=ja}} The museum was '''closed for renovations''' from '''December 24, 2018''', due to updates to the '''air conditioning system''' and concerns over the presence of '''harmful substances inside display cases'''. The institute has conducted '''excavations of major historical sites''' within Nara Prefecture, including the '''Makimuku Ruins''' ({{ill|Makimuku ruins|ja|纒向遺跡}}), {{ill|Hokenoyama Kofun|ja|ホケノ山古墳}}, {{ill|Kurozuka Kofun|ja|黒塚古墳}}, {{ill|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun|ja|桜井茶臼山古墳}}, {{ill|Mesuriyama Kofun|ja|メスリ山古墳}}, {{ill|Fujinoki Kofun|ja|藤ノ木古墳}}, and {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}}. It has gained '''national recognition''' for its work on '''excavations of ancient capitals''' such as '''Asuka-kyō''' ({{ill|Asuka-dera|ja|飛鳥京跡}}), '''Tōdai-ji''' ({{ill|Tōdai-ji|ja|東大寺}}), and '''Tōshōdai-ji''' ({{ill|Tōshōdai-ji|ja|唐招提寺}}). The institute also manages an '''affiliated museum''' and operates a '''cultural property storage and research center''' in {{ill|Uda, Nara|ja|宇陀市}}. == History == * '''1938 (Shōwa 13)''' - Archaeological surveys of '''Kashihara Shrine’s outer precincts''' were conducted as part of the '''2600th Anniversary of Japan’s Founding Project''' (紀元二千六百年記念行事). '''Masao Suenaga''' ({{ill|Masao Suenaga|ja|末永雅雄}}) from '''Kyoto University''' led the survey on '''September 13''', which later became the institute’s '''founding date'''. * '''1951 (Shōwa 26)''' - The '''Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute''' was officially established with '''Masao Suenaga''' as the first director. * '''1972 (Shōwa 47)''' - The '''Takamatsuzuka Kofun murals''' were discovered during an excavation by the institute. * '''1974 (Shōwa 49)''' - The former '''Kashihara Kōen Archaeological Museum''' became an '''affiliated facility''', later renamed '''Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum'''. * '''1985 (Shōwa 60)''' - First excavation of '''Fujinoki Kofun'''. * '''1990 (Heisei 2)''' - The institute’s '''new headquarters''' was completed. * '''1997 (Heisei 9)''' - Excavation of '''Kurozuka Kofun'''. * '''2014 (Heisei 26)''' - '''Cultural Property Storage and Research Center''' established in the former '''Nara Prefectural Murō High School'''. * '''2015 (Heisei 27)''' - The institute was transferred from the '''Nara Prefectural Board of Education''' to the '''Governor’s Office of Nara Prefecture'''. == Organization == * The '''Director-General''' oversees the '''Deputy Director''' and '''Museum Director'''. * The '''Deputy Director''' supervises: * '''Planning Division''' (企画部) * '''Planning Section''' (企画課) * '''Documentation Section''' (資料課) * '''Excavation Division''' (調査部) * '''Excavation Section''' (調査課) * '''General Affairs Section''' (総務課) * The '''Museum Director''' supervises the '''Deputy Museum Director''' and the '''Curatorial Section''' (学芸課). == Affiliated Museum == {{博物館 |名称 = Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum |画像 = Kashiharakouen-koukogaku02.jpg|Affiliated Museum |imagesize = 250 |正式名称 = 奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館 |専門分野 = {{ill|Archaeology|ja|考古学}}・{{ill|Buried Cultural Properties|ja|埋蔵文化財}} |所在地 = [[Nara Prefecture]] [[Kashihara, Nara]] |アクセス = [[Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu]] {{ill|Kintetsu Kashihara Line|ja|近鉄橿原線}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Yoshino Line|ja|近鉄吉野線}} - [[Kashiharajingū-mae Station]] (15-min walk)
[[Kintetsu Kashihara Line]] - [[Unebigoryōmae Station]] (5-min walk) |URL = [http://www.kashikoken.jp/museum/ Official Website] }} The '''museum houses approximately 3,700 artifacts''' spanning from the '''Paleolithic period to the Muromachi period'''.{{Cite web|和書|title=Nara's Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum Reopens|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211102110118/https://nordot.app/828174813890314240|website=Kyodo News|accessdate=2021-11-03|language=ja}} The museum was '''closed for renovations''' from '''December 24, 2018''', due to updates to the '''air conditioning system''' and concerns over the presence of '''harmful substances inside display cases'''. It '''reopened on November 3, 2021'''. == Related Topics == {{Commonscat|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture}} * {{ill|Category:People from Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|Category:奈良県立橿原考古学研究所の人物}} == External Links == * {{official|http://www.kashikoken.jp/}} * {{Facebook|kashikoken|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute}} {{coord|region:JP|display=title}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute}} [[Category:Museums in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Archaeological museums in Japan]] [[Category:Research institutes in Japan]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan (archaeological materials)]] [[Category:Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:Buildings and structures completed in 1980]] [[Category:Buildings and structures completed in 1990]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kashihara Archaeological Institute-qq]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:Https://de.wikipedia.org/wiki/]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Kashihara Archaeological Institute]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Kashihara Archaeological Institute]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kashihara Archaeological Institute]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kashihara Archaeological Institute]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kashihara Archaeological Institute]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]][[sn:Kashihara Archaeological Institute]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kashihara Archaeological Institute]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kashihara Archaeological Institute]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kashihara Archaeological Institute]][[Category:qq]] [[de:Arch%C3%A4ologisches Forschungsinstitut der Pr%C3%A4fektur Nara in Kashihara]] [[ja:%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E7%9C%8C%E7%AB%8B%E6%A9%BF%E5%8E%9F%E8%80%83%E5%8F%A4%E5%AD%A6%E7%A0%94%E7%A9%B6%E6%89%80]]

The institute was transferred from the '''Nara Prefectural Board of Education''' to the '''Governor’s Office of Nara Prefecture'''. == Organization == * The '''Director-General''' oversees the '''Deputy Director''' and '''Museum Director'''. * The '''Deputy Director''' supervises: * '''Planning Division''' (企画部) * '''Planning Section''' (企画課) * '''Documentation Section''' (資料課) * '''Excavation Division''' (調査部) * '''Excavation Section''' (調査課) * '''General Affairs Section''' (総務課) * The '''Museum Director''' supervises the '''Deputy Museum Director''' and the '''Curatorial Section''' (学芸課). == Affiliated Museum == {{博物館 |名称 = Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum |画像 = Kashiharakouen-koukogaku02.jpg|Affiliated Museum |imagesize = 250 |正式名称 = 奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館 |専門分野 = {{ill|Archaeology|ja|考古学}}・{{ill|Buried Cultural Properties|ja|埋蔵文化財}} |所在地 = [[Nara Prefecture]] [[Kashihara, Nara]] |アクセス = [[Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu]] {{ill|Kintetsu Kashihara Line|ja|近鉄橿原線}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Yoshino Line|ja|近鉄吉野線}} - [[Kashiharajingū-mae Station]] (15-min walk)
[[Kintetsu Kashihara Line]] - [[Unebigoryōmae Station]] (5-min walk) |URL = [http://www.kashikoken.jp/museum/ Official Website] }} The '''museum houses approximately 3,700 artifacts''' spanning from the '''Paleolithic period to the Muromachi period'''.{{Cite web|和書|title=Nara's Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum Reopens|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211102110118/https://nordot.app/828174813890314240|website=Kyodo News|accessdate=2021-11-03|language=ja}} The museum was '''closed for renovations''' from '''December 24, 2018''', due to updates to the '''air conditioning system''' and concerns over the presence of '''harmful substances inside display cases'''. It '''reopened on November 3, 2021'''. == Related Topics == {{Commonscat|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture}} * {{ill|Category:People from Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|Category:奈良県立橿原考古学研究所の人物}} == External Links == * {{official|http://www.kashikoken.jp/}} * {{Facebook|kashikoken|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kashihara Archaeological Institute-qq]] {{pp-vandalism|small=yes}} [[file:Kashihara Archaeological Institute , Nara prefecture.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute]] The '''Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute''' (奈良県立橿原考古学研究所, *Nara Kenritsu Kashihara Kōkogaku Kenkyūjo*) is a '''prefectural institution''' located in {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市}}, specializing in the research and investigation of '''buried cultural properties''' (埋蔵文化財). It is an institution under the jurisdiction of '''Nara Prefecture'''. As one of the oldest '''public institutions for archaeological research''' still in operation today, it is sometimes abbreviated as '''Kashikōken''' (橿考研). The institute has conducted '''excavations of major historical sites''' within Nara Prefecture, including the '''Makimuku Ruins''' ({{ill|Makimuku ruins|ja|纒向遺跡}}), {{ill|Hokenoyama Kofun|ja|ホケノ山古墳}}, {{ill|Kurozuka Kofun|ja|黒塚古墳}}, {{ill|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun|ja|桜井茶臼山古墳}}, {{ill|Mesuriyama Kofun|ja|メスリ山古墳}}, {{ill|Fujinoki Kofun|ja|藤ノ木古墳}}, and {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}}. It has gained '''national recognition''' for its work on '''excavations of ancient capitals''' such as '''Asuka-kyō''' ({{ill|Asuka-dera|ja|飛鳥京跡}}), '''Tōdai-ji''' ({{ill|Tōdai-ji|ja|東大寺}}), and '''Tōshōdai-ji''' ({{ill|Tōshōdai-ji|ja|唐招提寺}}). The institute also manages an '''affiliated museum''' and operates a '''cultural property storage and research center''' in {{ill|Uda, Nara|ja|宇陀市}}. == History == * '''1938 (Shōwa 13)''' - Archaeological surveys of '''Kashihara Shrine’s outer precincts''' were conducted as part of the '''2600th Anniversary of Japan’s Founding Project''' (紀元二千六百年記念行事). '''Masao Suenaga''' ({{ill|Masao Suenaga|ja|末永雅雄}}) from '''Kyoto University''' led the survey on '''September 13''', which later became the institute’s '''founding date'''. * '''1951 (Shōwa 26)''' - The '''Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute''' was officially established with '''Masao Suenaga''' as the first director. * '''1972 (Shōwa 47)''' - The '''Takamatsuzuka Kofun murals''' were discovered during an excavation by the institute. * '''1974 (Shōwa 49)''' - The former '''Kashihara Kōen Archaeological Museum''' became an '''affiliated facility''', later renamed '''Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum'''. * '''1985 (Shōwa 60)''' - First excavation of '''Fujinoki Kofun'''. * '''1990 (Heisei 2)''' - The institute’s '''new headquarters''' was completed. * '''1997 (Heisei 9)''' - Excavation of '''Kurozuka Kofun'''. * '''2014 (Heisei 26)''' - '''Cultural Property Storage and Research Center''' established in the former '''Nara Prefectural Murō High School'''. * '''2015 (Heisei 27)''' - The institute was transferred from the '''Nara Prefectural Board of Education''' to the '''Governor’s Office of Nara Prefecture'''. == Organization == * The '''Director-General''' oversees the '''Deputy Director''' and '''Museum Director'''. * The '''Deputy Director''' supervises: * '''Planning Division''' (企画部) * '''Planning Section''' (企画課) * '''Documentation Section''' (資料課) * '''Excavation Division''' (調査部) * '''Excavation Section''' (調査課) * '''General Affairs Section''' (総務課) * The '''Museum Director''' supervises the '''Deputy Museum Director''' and the '''Curatorial Section''' (学芸課). == Affiliated Museum == {{博物館 |名称 = Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum |画像 = Kashiharakouen-koukogaku02.jpg|Affiliated Museum |imagesize = 250 |正式名称 = 奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館 |専門分野 = {{ill|Archaeology|ja|考古学}}・{{ill|Buried Cultural Properties|ja|埋蔵文化財}} |所在地 = [[Nara Prefecture]] [[Kashihara, Nara]] |アクセス = [[Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu]] {{ill|Kintetsu Kashihara Line|ja|近鉄橿原線}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Yoshino Line|ja|近鉄吉野線}} - [[Kashiharajingū-mae Station]] (15-min walk)
[[Kintetsu Kashihara Line]] - [[Unebigoryōmae Station]] (5-min walk) |URL = [http://www.kashikoken.jp/museum/ Official Website] }} The '''museum houses approximately 3,700 artifacts''' spanning from the '''Paleolithic period to the Muromachi period'''.{{Cite web|和書|title=Nara's Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum Reopens|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211102110118/https://nordot.app/828174813890314240|website=Kyodo News|accessdate=2021-11-03|language=ja}} The museum was '''closed for renovations''' from '''December 24, 2018''', due to updates to the '''air conditioning system''' and concerns over the presence of '''harmful substances inside display cases'''. It '''reopened on November 3, 2021'''. == Related Topics == {{Commonscat|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture}} * {{ill|Category:People from Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|Category:奈良県立橿原考古学研究所の人物}} == External Links == * {{official|http://www.kashikoken.jp/}} * {{Facebook|kashikoken|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kashihara Archaeological Institute-qq]] {{pp-vandalism|small=yes}} [[file:Kashihara Archaeological Institute , Nara prefecture.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute]] The '''Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute''' (奈良県立橿原考古学研究所, *Nara Kenritsu Kashihara Kōkogaku Kenkyūjo*) is a '''prefectural institution''' located in {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市}}, specializing in the research and investigation of '''buried cultural properties''' (埋蔵文化財). It is an institution under the jurisdiction of '''Nara Prefecture'''. As one of the oldest '''public institutions for archaeological research''' still in operation today, it is sometimes abbreviated as '''Kashikōken''' (橿考研). The institute has conducted '''excavations of major historical sites''' within Nara Prefecture, including the '''Makimuku Ruins''' ({{ill|Makimuku ruins|ja|纒向遺跡}}), {{ill|Hokenoyama Kofun|ja|ホケノ山古墳}}, {{ill|Kurozuka Kofun|ja|黒塚古墳}}, {{ill|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun|ja|桜井茶臼山古墳}}, {{ill|Mesuriyama Kofun|ja|メスリ山古墳}}, {{ill|Fujinoki Kofun|ja|藤ノ木古墳}}, and {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}}. It has gained '''national recognition''' for its work on '''excavations of ancient capitals''' such as '''Asuka-kyō''' ({{ill|Asuka-dera|ja|飛鳥京跡}}), '''Tōdai-ji''' ({{ill|Tōdai-ji|ja|東大寺}}), and '''Tōshōdai-ji''' ({{ill|Tōshōdai-ji|ja|唐招提寺}}). The institute also manages an '''affiliated museum''' and operates a '''cultural property storage and research center''' in {{ill|Uda, Nara|ja|宇陀市}}. == History == * '''1938 (Shōwa 13)''' - Archaeological surveys of '''Kashihara Shrine’s outer precincts''' were conducted as part of the '''2600th Anniversary of Japan’s Founding Project''' (紀元二千六百年記念行事). '''Masao Suenaga''' ({{ill|Masao Suenaga|ja|末永雅雄}}) from '''Kyoto University''' led the survey on '''September 13''', which later became the institute’s '''founding date'''. * '''1951 (Shōwa 26)''' - The '''Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute''' was officially established with '''Masao Suenaga''' as the first director. * '''1972 (Shōwa 47)''' - The '''Takamatsuzuka Kofun murals''' were discovered during an excavation by the institute. * '''1974 (Shōwa 49)''' - The former '''Kashihara Kōen Archaeological Museum''' became an '''affiliated facility''', later renamed '''Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum'''. * '''1985 (Shōwa 60)''' - First excavation of '''Fujinoki Kofun'''. * '''1990 (Heisei 2)''' - The institute’s '''new headquarters''' was completed. * '''1997 (Heisei 9)''' - Excavation of '''Kurozuka Kofun'''. * '''2014 (Heisei 26)''' - '''Cultural Property Storage and Research Center''' established in the former '''Nara Prefectural Murō High School'''. * '''2015 (Heisei 27)''' - The institute was transferred from the '''Nara Prefectural Board of Education''' to the '''Governor’s Office of Nara Prefecture'''. == Organization == * The '''Director-General''' oversees the '''Deputy Director''' and '''Museum Director'''. * The '''Deputy Director''' supervises: * '''Planning Division''' (企画部) * '''Planning Section''' (企画課) * '''Documentation Section''' (資料課) * '''Excavation Division''' (調査部) * '''Excavation Section''' (調査課) * '''General Affairs Section''' (総務課) * The '''Museum Director''' supervises the '''Deputy Museum Director''' and the '''Curatorial Section''' (学芸課). == Affiliated Museum == {{博物館 |名称 = Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum |画像 = Kashiharakouen-koukogaku02.jpg|Affiliated Museum |imagesize = 250 |正式名称 = 奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館 |専門分野 = {{ill|Archaeology|ja|考古学}}・{{ill|Buried Cultural Properties|ja|埋蔵文化財}} |所在地 = [[Nara Prefecture]] [[Kashihara, Nara]] |アクセス = [[Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu]] {{ill|Kintetsu Kashihara Line|ja|近鉄橿原線}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Yoshino Line|ja|近鉄吉野線}} - [[Kashiharajingū-mae Station]] (15-min walk)
[[Kintetsu Kashihara Line]] - [[Unebigoryōmae Station]] (5-min walk) |URL = [http://www.kashikoken.jp/museum/ Official Website] }} The '''museum houses approximately 3,700 artifacts''' spanning from the '''Paleolithic period to the Muromachi period'''.{{Cite web|和書|title=Nara's Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum Reopens|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211102110118/https://nordot.app/828174813890314240|website=Kyodo News|accessdate=2021-11-03|language=ja}} The museum was '''closed for renovations''' from '''December 24, 2018''', due to updates to the '''air conditioning system''' and concerns over the presence of '''harmful substances inside display cases'''. It '''reopened on November 3, 2021'''. == Related Topics == {{Commonscat|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture}} * {{ill|Category:People from Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|Category:奈良県立橿原考古学研究所の人物}} == External Links == * {{official|http://www.kashikoken.jp/}} * {{Facebook|kashikoken|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kashihara Archaeological Institute-qq]] {{pp-vandalism|small=yes}} [[file:Kashihara Archaeological Institute , Nara prefecture.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute]] The '''Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute''' (奈良県立橿原考古学研究所, *Nara Kenritsu Kashihara Kōkogaku Kenkyūjo*) is a '''prefectural institution''' located in {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市}}, specializing in the research and investigation of '''buried cultural properties''' (埋蔵文化財). It is an institution under the jurisdiction of '''Nara Prefecture'''. As one of the oldest '''public institutions for archaeological research''' still in operation today, it is sometimes abbreviated as '''Kashikōken''' (橿考研). The institute has conducted '''excavations of major historical sites''' within Nara Prefecture, including the '''Makimuku Ruins''' ({{ill|Makimuku ruins|ja|纒向遺跡}}), {{ill|Hokenoyama Kofun|ja|ホケノ山古墳}}, {{ill|Kurozuka Kofun|ja|黒塚古墳}}, {{ill|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun|ja|桜井茶臼山古墳}}, {{ill|Mesuriyama Kofun|ja|メスリ山古墳}}, {{ill|Fujinoki Kofun|ja|藤ノ木古墳}}, and {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}}. It has gained '''national recognition''' for its work on '''excavations of ancient capitals''' such as '''Asuka-kyō''' ({{ill|Asuka-dera|ja|飛鳥京跡}}), '''Tōdai-ji''' ({{ill|Tōdai-ji|ja|東大寺}}), and '''Tōshōdai-ji''' ({{ill|Tōshōdai-ji|ja|唐招提寺}}). The institute also manages an '''affiliated museum''' and operates a '''cultural property storage and research center''' in {{ill|Uda, Nara|ja|宇陀市}}. == History == * '''1938 (Shōwa 13)''' - Archaeological surveys of '''Kashihara Shrine’s outer precincts''' were conducted as part of the '''2600th Anniversary of Japan’s Founding Project''' (紀元二千六百年記念行事). '''Masao Suenaga''' ({{ill|Masao Suenaga|ja|末永雅雄}}) from '''Kyoto University''' led the survey on '''September 13''', which later became the institute’s '''founding date'''. * '''1951 (Shōwa 26)''' - The '''Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute''' was officially established with '''Masao Suenaga''' as the first director. * '''1972 (Shōwa 47)''' - The '''Takamatsuzuka Kofun murals''' were discovered during an excavation by the institute. * '''1974 (Shōwa 49)''' - The former '''Kashihara Kōen Archaeological Museum''' became an '''affiliated facility''', later renamed '''Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum'''. * '''1985 (Shōwa 60)''' - First excavation of '''Fujinoki Kofun'''. * '''1990 (Heisei 2)''' - The institute’s '''new headquarters''' was completed. * '''1997 (Heisei 9)''' - Excavation of '''Kurozuka Kofun'''. * '''2014 (Heisei 26)''' - '''Cultural Property Storage and Research Center''' established in the former '''Nara Prefectural Murō High School'''. * '''2015 (Heisei 27)''' - The institute was transferred from the '''Nara Prefectural Board of Education''' to the '''Governor’s Office of Nara Prefecture'''. == Organization == * The '''Director-General''' oversees the '''Deputy Director''' and '''Museum Director'''. * The '''Deputy Director''' supervises: * '''Planning Division''' (企画部) * '''Planning Section''' (企画課) * '''Documentation Section''' (資料課) * '''Excavation Division''' (調査部) * '''Excavation Section''' (調査課) * '''General Affairs Section''' (総務課) * The '''Museum Director''' supervises the '''Deputy Museum Director''' and the '''Curatorial Section''' (学芸課). == Affiliated Museum == {{博物館 |名称 = Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum |画像 = Kashiharakouen-koukogaku02.jpg|Affiliated Museum |imagesize = 250 |正式名称 = 奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館 |専門分野 = {{ill|Archaeology|ja|考古学}}・{{ill|Buried Cultural Properties|ja|埋蔵文化財}} |所在地 = [[Nara Prefecture]] [[Kashihara, Nara]] |アクセス = [[Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu]] {{ill|Kintetsu Kashihara Line|ja|近鉄橿原線}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Yoshino Line|ja|近鉄吉野線}} - [[Kashiharajingū-mae Station]] (15-min walk)
[[Kintetsu Kashihara Line]] - [[Unebigoryōmae Station]] (5-min walk) |URL = [http://www.kashikoken.jp/museum/ Official Website] }} The '''museum houses approximately 3,700 artifacts''' spanning from the '''Paleolithic period to the Muromachi period'''.{{Cite web|和書|title=Nara's Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum Reopens|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211102110118/https://nordot.app/828174813890314240|website=Kyodo News|accessdate=2021-11-03|language=ja}} The museum was '''closed for renovations''' from '''December 24, 2018''', due to updates to the '''air conditioning system''' and concerns over the presence of '''harmful substances inside display cases'''. It '''reopened on November 3, 2021'''. == Related Topics == {{Commonscat|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture}} * {{ill|Category:People from Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|Category:奈良県立橿原考古学研究所の人物}} == External Links == * {{official|http://www.kashikoken.jp/}} * {{Facebook|kashikoken|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kashihara Archaeological Institute-qq]] {{pp-vandalism|small=yes}} [[file:Kashihara Archaeological Institute , Nara prefecture.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute]] The '''Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute''' (奈良県立橿原考古学研究所, *Nara Kenritsu Kashihara Kōkogaku Kenkyūjo*) is a '''prefectural institution''' located in {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市}}, specializing in the research and investigation of '''buried cultural properties''' (埋蔵文化財). It is an institution under the jurisdiction of '''Nara Prefecture'''. As one of the oldest '''public institutions for archaeological research''' still in operation today, it is sometimes abbreviated as '''Kashikōken''' (橿考研). The institute has conducted '''excavations of major historical sites''' within Nara Prefecture, including the '''Makimuku Ruins''' ({{ill|Makimuku ruins|ja|纒向遺跡}}), {{ill|Hokenoyama Kofun|ja|ホケノ山古墳}}, {{ill|Kurozuka Kofun|ja|黒塚古墳}}, {{ill|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun|ja|桜井茶臼山古墳}}, {{ill|Mesuriyama Kofun|ja|メスリ山古墳}}, {{ill|Fujinoki Kofun|ja|藤ノ木古墳}}, and {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}}. It has gained '''national recognition''' for its work on '''excavations of ancient capitals''' such as '''Asuka-kyō''' ({{ill|Asuka-dera|ja|飛鳥京跡}}), '''Tōdai-ji''' ({{ill|Tōdai-ji|ja|東大寺}}), and '''Tōshōdai-ji''' ({{ill|Tōshōdai-ji|ja|唐招提寺}}). The institute also manages an '''affiliated museum''' and operates a '''cultural property storage and research center''' in {{ill|Uda, Nara|ja|宇陀市}}. == History == * '''1938 (Shōwa 13)''' - Archaeological surveys of '''Kashihara Shrine’s outer precincts''' were conducted as part of the '''2600th Anniversary of Japan’s Founding Project''' (紀元二千六百年記念行事). '''Masao Suenaga''' ({{ill|Masao Suenaga|ja|末永雅雄}}) from '''Kyoto University''' led the survey on '''September 13''', which later became the institute’s '''founding date'''. * '''1951 (Shōwa 26)''' - The '''Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute''' was officially established with '''Masao Suenaga''' as the first director. * '''1972 (Shōwa 47)''' - The '''Takamatsuzuka Kofun murals''' were discovered during an excavation by the institute. * '''1974 (Shōwa 49)''' - The former '''Kashihara Kōen Archaeological Museum''' became an '''affiliated facility''', later renamed '''Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum'''. * '''1985 (Shōwa 60)''' - First excavation of '''Fujinoki Kofun'''. * '''1990 (Heisei 2)''' - The institute’s '''new headquarters''' was completed. * '''1997 (Heisei 9)''' - Excavation of '''Kurozuka Kofun'''. * '''2014 (Heisei 26)''' - '''Cultural Property Storage and Research Center''' established in the former '''Nara Prefectural Murō High School'''. * '''2015 (Heisei 27)''' - The institute was transferred from the '''Nara Prefectural Board of Education''' to the '''Governor’s Office of Nara Prefecture'''. == Organization == * The '''Director-General''' oversees the '''Deputy Director''' and '''Museum Director'''. * The '''Deputy Director''' supervises: * '''Planning Division''' (企画部) * '''Planning Section''' (企画課) * '''Documentation Section''' (資料課) * '''Excavation Division''' (調査部) * '''Excavation Section''' (調査課) * '''General Affairs Section''' (総務課) * The '''Museum Director''' supervises the '''Deputy Museum Director''' and the '''Curatorial Section''' (学芸課). == Affiliated Museum == {{博物館 |名称 = Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum |画像 = Kashiharakouen-koukogaku02.jpg|Affiliated Museum |imagesize = 250 |正式名称 = 奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館 |専門分野 = {{ill|Archaeology|ja|考古学}}・{{ill|Buried Cultural Properties|ja|埋蔵文化財}} |所在地 = [[Nara Prefecture]] [[Kashihara, Nara]] |アクセス = [[Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu]] {{ill|Kintetsu Kashihara Line|ja|近鉄橿原線}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Yoshino Line|ja|近鉄吉野線}} - [[Kashiharajingū-mae Station]] (15-min walk)
[[Kintetsu Kashihara Line]] - [[Unebigoryōmae Station]] (5-min walk) |URL = [http://www.kashikoken.jp/museum/ Official Website] }} The '''museum houses approximately 3,700 artifacts''' spanning from the '''Paleolithic period to the Muromachi period'''.{{Cite web|和書|title=Nara's Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum Reopens|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211102110118/https://nordot.app/828174813890314240|website=Kyodo News|accessdate=2021-11-03|language=ja}} The museum was '''closed for renovations''' from '''December 24, 2018''', due to updates to the '''air conditioning system''' and concerns over the presence of '''harmful substances inside display cases'''. It '''reopened on November 3, 2021'''. == Related Topics == {{Commonscat|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture}} * {{ill|Category:People from Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|Category:奈良県立橿原考古学研究所の人物}} == External Links == * {{official|http://www.kashikoken.jp/}} * {{Facebook|kashikoken|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kashihara Archaeological Institute-qq]] {{pp-vandalism|small=yes}} [[file:Kashihara Archaeological Institute , Nara prefecture.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute]] The '''Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute''' (奈良県立橿原考古学研究所, *Nara Kenritsu Kashihara Kōkogaku Kenkyūjo*) is a '''prefectural institution''' located in {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市}}, specializing in the research and investigation of '''buried cultural properties''' (埋蔵文化財). It is an institution under the jurisdiction of '''Nara Prefecture'''. As one of the oldest '''public institutions for archaeological research''' still in operation today, it is sometimes abbreviated as '''Kashikōken''' (橿考研). The institute has conducted '''excavations of major historical sites''' within Nara Prefecture, including the '''Makimuku Ruins''' ({{ill|Makimuku ruins|ja|纒向遺跡}}), {{ill|Hokenoyama Kofun|ja|ホケノ山古墳}}, {{ill|Kurozuka Kofun|ja|黒塚古墳}}, {{ill|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun|ja|桜井茶臼山古墳}}, {{ill|Mesuriyama Kofun|ja|メスリ山古墳}}, {{ill|Fujinoki Kofun|ja|藤ノ木古墳}}, and {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}}. It has gained '''national recognition''' for its work on '''excavations of ancient capitals''' such as '''Asuka-kyō''' ({{ill|Asuka-dera|ja|飛鳥京跡}}), '''Tōdai-ji''' ({{ill|Tōdai-ji|ja|東大寺}}), and '''Tōshōdai-ji''' ({{ill|Tōshōdai-ji|ja|唐招提寺}}). The institute also manages an '''affiliated museum''' and operates a '''cultural property storage and research center''' in {{ill|Uda, Nara|ja|宇陀市}}. == History == * '''1938 (Shōwa 13)''' - Archaeological surveys of '''Kashihara Shrine’s outer precincts''' were conducted as part of the '''2600th Anniversary of Japan’s Founding Project''' (紀元二千六百年記念行事). '''Masao Suenaga''' ({{ill|Masao Suenaga|ja|末永雅雄}}) from '''Kyoto University''' led the survey on '''September 13''', which later became the institute’s '''founding date'''. * '''1951 (Shōwa 26)''' - The '''Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute''' was officially established with '''Masao Suenaga''' as the first director. * '''1972 (Shōwa 47)''' - The '''Takamatsuzuka Kofun murals''' were discovered during an excavation by the institute. * '''1974 (Shōwa 49)''' - The former '''Kashihara Kōen Archaeological Museum''' became an '''affiliated facility''', later renamed '''Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum'''. * '''1985 (Shōwa 60)''' - First excavation of '''Fujinoki Kofun'''. * '''1990 (Heisei 2)''' - The institute’s '''new headquarters''' was completed. * '''1997 (Heisei 9)''' - Excavation of '''Kurozuka Kofun'''. * '''2014 (Heisei 26)''' - '''Cultural Property Storage and Research Center''' established in the former '''Nara Prefectural Murō High School'''. * '''2015 (Heisei 27)''' - The institute was transferred from the '''Nara Prefectural Board of Education''' to the '''Governor’s Office of Nara Prefecture'''. == Organization == * The '''Director-General''' oversees the '''Deputy Director''' and '''Museum Director'''. * The '''Deputy Director''' supervises: * '''Planning Division''' (企画部) * '''Planning Section''' (企画課) * '''Documentation Section''' (資料課) * '''Excavation Division''' (調査部) * '''Excavation Section''' (調査課) * '''General Affairs Section''' (総務課) * The '''Museum Director''' supervises the '''Deputy Museum Director''' and the '''Curatorial Section''' (学芸課). == Affiliated Museum == {{博物館 |名称 = Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum |image = Kashiharakouen-koukogaku02.jpg|Affiliated Museum |imagesize = 250 |正式名称 = 奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館 |専門分野 = {{ill|Archaeology|ja|考古学}}・{{ill|Buried Cultural Properties|ja|埋蔵文化財}} |所在地 = [[Nara Prefecture]] [[Kashihara, Nara]] |アクセス = [[Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu]] {{ill|Kintetsu Kashihara Line|ja|近鉄橿原線}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Yoshino Line|ja|近鉄吉野線}} - [[Kashiharajingū-mae Station]] (15-min walk)
[[Kintetsu Kashihara Line]] - [[Unebigoryōmae Station]] (5-min walk) |URL = [http://www.kashikoken.jp/museum/ Official Website] }} The '''museum houses approximately 3,700 artifacts''' spanning from the '''Paleolithic period to the Muromachi period'''.{{Cite web|和書|title=Nara's Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum Reopens|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211102110118/https://nordot.app/828174813890314240|website=Kyodo News|accessdate=2021-11-03|language=ja}} The museum was '''closed for renovations''' from '''December 24, 2018''', due to updates to the '''air conditioning system''' and concerns over the presence of '''harmful substances inside display cases'''. It '''reopened on November 3, 2021'''. == See Also == {{Commonscat|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture}} * {{ill|Category:People from Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|Category:奈良県立橿原考古学研究所の人物}} == External Links == * {{official|http://www.kashikoken.jp/}} * {{Facebook|kashikoken|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq deprecated open]][[Category:qq deprecated close]] {{pp-vandalism|small=yes}} [[file:Kashihara Archaeological Institute , Nara prefecture.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute]] The '''Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute''' (奈良県立橿原考古学研究所, *Nara Kenritsu Kashihara Kōkogaku Kenkyūjo*) is a '''prefectural institution''' located in {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市}}, specializing in the research and investigation of '''buried cultural properties''' (埋蔵文化財). It is an institution under the jurisdiction of '''Nara Prefecture'''. As one of the oldest '''public institutions for archaeological research''' still in operation today, it is sometimes abbreviated as '''Kashikōken''' (橿考研). The institute has conducted '''excavations of major historical sites''' within Nara Prefecture, including the '''Makimuku Ruins''' ({{ill|Makimuku ruins|ja|纒向遺跡}}), {{ill|Hokenoyama Kofun|ja|ホケノ山古墳}}, {{ill|Kurozuka Kofun|ja|黒塚古墳}}, {{ill|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun|ja|桜井茶臼山古墳}}, {{ill|Mesuriyama Kofun|ja|メスリ山古墳}}, {{ill|Fujinoki Kofun|ja|藤ノ木古墳}}, and {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}}. It has gained '''national recognition''' for its work on '''excavations of ancient capitals''' such as '''Asuka-kyō''' ({{ill|Asuka-dera|ja|飛鳥京跡}}), '''Tōdai-ji''' ({{ill|Tōdai-ji|ja|東大寺}}), and '''Tōshōdai-ji''' ({{ill|Tōshōdai-ji|ja|唐招提寺}}). The institute also manages an '''affiliated museum''' and operates a '''cultural property storage and research center''' in {{ill|Uda, Nara|ja|宇陀市}}. == History == * '''1938 (Shōwa 13)''' - Archaeological surveys of '''Kashihara Shrine’s outer precincts''' were conducted as part of the '''2600th Anniversary of Japan’s Founding Project''' (紀元二千六百年記念行事). '''Masao Suenaga''' ({{ill|Masao Suenaga|ja|末永雅雄}}) from '''Kyoto University''' led the survey on '''September 13''', which later became the institute’s '''founding date'''. * '''1951 (Shōwa 26)''' - The '''Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute''' was officially established with '''Masao Suenaga''' as the first director. * '''1972 (Shōwa 47)''' - The '''Takamatsuzuka Kofun murals''' were discovered during an excavation by the institute. * '''1974 (Shōwa 49)''' - The former '''Kashihara Kōen Archaeological Museum''' became an '''affiliated facility''', later renamed '''Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum'''. * '''1985 (Shōwa 60)''' - First excavation of '''Fujinoki Kofun'''. * '''1990 (Heisei 2)''' - The institute’s '''new headquarters''' was completed. * '''1997 (Heisei 9)''' - Excavation of '''Kurozuka Kofun'''. * '''2014 (Heisei 26)''' - '''Cultural Property Storage and Research Center''' established in the former '''Nara Prefectural Murō High School'''. * '''2015 (Heisei 27)''' - The institute was transferred from the '''Nara Prefectural Board of Education''' to the '''Governor’s Office of Nara Prefecture'''. == Organization == * The '''Director-General''' oversees the '''Deputy Director''' and '''Museum Director'''. * The '''Deputy Director''' supervises: * '''Planning Division''' (企画部) * '''Planning Section''' (企画課) * '''Documentation Section''' (資料課) * '''Excavation Division''' (調査部) * '''Excavation Section''' (調査課) * '''General Affairs Section''' (総務課) * The '''Museum Director''' supervises the '''Deputy Museum Director''' and the '''Curatorial Section''' (学芸課). == Affiliated Museum == {{博物館 |名称 = Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum |image = Kashiharakouen-koukogaku02.jpg|Affiliated Museum |imagesize = 250 |正式名称 = 奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館 |専門分野 = {{ill|Archaeology|ja|考古学}}・{{ill|Buried Cultural Properties|ja|埋蔵文化財}} |所在地 = [[Nara Prefecture]] [[Kashihara, Nara]] |アクセス = [[Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu]] {{ill|Kintetsu Kashihara Line|ja|近鉄橿原線}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Yoshino Line|ja|近鉄吉野線}} - [[Kashiharajingū-mae Station]] (15-min walk)
[[Kintetsu Kashihara Line]] - [[Unebigoryōmae Station]] (5-min walk) |URL = [http://www.kashikoken.jp/museum/ Official Website] }} The '''museum houses approximately 3,700 artifacts''' spanning from the '''Paleolithic period to the Muromachi period'''.{{Cite web|和書|title=Nara's Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum Reopens|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211102110118/https://nordot.app/828174813890314240|website=Kyodo News|accessdate=2021-11-03|language=ja}} The museum was '''closed for renovations''' from '''December 24, 2018''', due to updates to the '''air conditioning system''' and concerns over the presence of '''harmful substances inside display cases'''. It '''reopened on November 3, 2021'''. == See Also == {{Commonscat|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture}} * {{ill|Category:People from Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|Category:奈良県立橿原考古学研究所の人物}} == External Links == * {{official|http://www.kashikoken.jp/}} * {{Facebook|kashikoken|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq deprecated open]] {{pp-vandalism|small=yes}} [[file:Kashihara Archaeological Institute , Nara prefecture.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute]] The '''Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute''' (奈良県立橿原考古学研究所, *Nara Kenritsu Kashihara Kōkogaku Kenkyūjo*) is a '''prefectural institution''' located in {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市}}, specializing in the research and investigation of '''buried cultural properties''' (埋蔵文化財). It is an institution under the jurisdiction of '''Nara Prefecture'''. As one of the oldest '''public institutions for archaeological research''' still in operation today, it is sometimes abbreviated as '''Kashikōken''' (橿考研). The institute has conducted '''excavations of major historical sites''' within Nara Prefecture, including the '''Makimuku Ruins''' ({{ill|Makimuku ruins|ja|纒向遺跡}}), {{ill|Hokenoyama Kofun|ja|ホケノ山古墳}}, {{ill|Kurozuka Kofun|ja|黒塚古墳}}, {{ill|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun|ja|桜井茶臼山古墳}}, {{ill|Mesuriyama Kofun|ja|メスリ山古墳}}, {{ill|Fujinoki Kofun|ja|藤ノ木古墳}}, and {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}}. It has gained '''national recognition''' for its work on '''excavations of ancient capitals''' such as '''Asuka-kyō''' ({{ill|Asuka-dera|ja|飛鳥京跡}}), '''Tōdai-ji''' ({{ill|Tōdai-ji|ja|東大寺}}), and '''Tōshōdai-ji''' ({{ill|Tōshōdai-ji|ja|唐招提寺}}). The institute also manages an '''affiliated museum''' and operates a '''cultural property storage and research center''' in {{ill|Uda, Nara|ja|宇陀市}}. == History == * '''1938 (Shōwa 13)''' - Archaeological surveys of '''Kashihara Shrine’s outer precincts''' were conducted as part of the '''2600th Anniversary of Japan’s Founding Project''' (紀元二千六百年記念行事). '''Masao Suenaga''' ({{ill|Masao Suenaga|ja|末永雅雄}}) from '''Kyoto University''' led the survey on '''September 13''', which later became the institute’s '''founding date'''. * '''1951 (Shōwa 26)''' - The '''Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute''' was officially established with '''Masao Suenaga''' as the first director. * '''1972 (Shōwa 47)''' - The '''Takamatsuzuka Kofun murals''' were discovered during an excavation by the institute. * '''1974 (Shōwa 49)''' - The former '''Kashihara Kōen Archaeological Museum''' became an '''affiliated facility''', later renamed '''Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum'''. * '''1985 (Shōwa 60)''' - First excavation of '''Fujinoki Kofun'''. * '''1990 (Heisei 2)''' - The institute’s '''new headquarters''' was completed. * '''1997 (Heisei 9)''' - Excavation of '''Kurozuka Kofun'''. * '''2014 (Heisei 26)''' - '''Cultural Property Storage and Research Center''' established in the former '''Nara Prefectural Murō High School'''. * '''2015 (Heisei 27)''' - The institute was transferred from the '''Nara Prefectural Board of Education''' to the '''Governor’s Office of Nara Prefecture'''. == Organization == * The '''Director-General''' oversees the '''Deputy Director''' and '''Museum Director'''. * The '''Deputy Director''' supervises: * '''Planning Division''' (企画部) * '''Planning Section''' (企画課) * '''Documentation Section''' (資料課) * '''Excavation Division''' (調査部) * '''Excavation Section''' (調査課) * '''General Affairs Section''' (総務課) * The '''Museum Director''' supervises the '''Deputy Museum Director''' and the '''Curatorial Section''' (学芸課). == Affiliated Museum == {{博物館 |名称 = Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum |image = Kashiharakouen-koukogaku02.jpg|Affiliated Museum |imagesize = 250 |正式名称 = 奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館 |専門分野 = {{ill|Archaeology|ja|考古学}}・{{ill|Buried Cultural Properties|ja|埋蔵文化財}} |所在地 = [[Nara Prefecture]] [[Kashihara, Nara]] |アクセス = [[Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu]] {{ill|Kintetsu Kashihara Line|ja|近鉄橿原線}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Yoshino Line|ja|近鉄吉野線}} - [[Kashiharajingū-mae Station]] (15-min walk)
[[Kintetsu Kashihara Line]] - [[Unebigoryōmae Station]] (5-min walk) |URL = [http://www.kashikoken.jp/museum/ Official Website] }} The '''museum houses approximately 3,700 artifacts''' spanning from the '''Paleolithic period to the Muromachi period'''.{{Cite web|和書|title=Nara's Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum Reopens|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211102110118/https://nordot.app/828174813890314240|website=Kyodo News|accessdate=2021-11-03|language=ja}} The museum was '''closed for renovations''' from '''December 24, 2018''', due to updates to the '''air conditioning system''' and concerns over the presence of '''harmful substances inside display cases'''. It '''reopened on November 3, 2021'''. == See Also == {{Commonscat|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture}} * {{ill|Category:People from Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|Category:奈良県立橿原考古学研究所の人物}} == External Links == * {{official|http://www.kashikoken.jp/}} * {{Facebook|kashikoken|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute}} {{coord|region:JP|display=title}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute}} [[Category:Museums in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Archaeological museums in Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] {{pp-vandalism|small=yes}} [[file:Kashihara Archaeological Institute , Nara prefecture.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute]] The '''Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute''' (奈良県立橿原考古学研究所, *Nara Kenritsu Kashihara Kōkogaku Kenkyūjo*) is a '''prefectural institution''' located in {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市}}, specializing in the research and investigation of '''buried cultural properties''' (埋蔵文化財). It is an institution under the jurisdiction of '''Nara Prefecture'''. As one of the oldest '''public institutions for archaeological research''' still in operation today, it is sometimes abbreviated as '''Kashikōken''' (橿考研). The institute has conducted '''excavations of major historical sites''' within Nara Prefecture, including the '''Makimuku Ruins''' ({{ill|Makimuku ruins|ja|纒向遺跡}}), {{ill|Hokenoyama Kofun|ja|ホケノ山古墳}}, {{ill|Kurozuka Kofun|ja|黒塚古墳}}, {{ill|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun|ja|桜井茶臼山古墳}}, {{ill|Mesuriyama Kofun|ja|メスリ山古墳}}, {{ill|Fujinoki Kofun|ja|藤ノ木古墳}}, and {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}}. It has gained '''national recognition''' for its work on '''excavations of ancient capitals''' such as '''Asuka-kyō''' ({{ill|Asuka-dera|ja|飛鳥京跡}}), '''Tōdai-ji''' ({{ill|Tōdai-ji|ja|東大寺}}), and '''Tōshōdai-ji''' ({{ill|Tōshōdai-ji|ja|唐招提寺}}). The institute also manages an '''affiliated museum''' and operates a '''cultural property storage and research center''' in {{ill|Uda, Nara|ja|宇陀市}}. == History == * '''1938 (Shōwa 13)''' - Archaeological surveys of '''Kashihara Shrine’s outer precincts''' were conducted as part of the '''2600th Anniversary of Japan’s Founding Project''' (紀元二千六百年記念行事). '''Masao Suenaga''' ({{ill|Masao Suenaga|ja|末永雅雄}}) from '''Kyoto University''' led the survey on '''September 13''', which later became the institute’s '''founding date'''. * '''1951 (Shōwa 26)''' - The '''Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute''' was officially established with '''Masao Suenaga''' as the first director. * '''1972 (Shōwa 47)''' - The '''Takamatsuzuka Kofun murals''' were discovered during an excavation by the institute. * '''1974 (Shōwa 49)''' - The former '''Kashihara Kōen Archaeological Museum''' became an '''affiliated facility''', later renamed '''Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum'''. * '''1985 (Shōwa 60)''' - First excavation of '''Fujinoki Kofun'''. * '''1990 (Heisei 2)''' - The institute’s '''new headquarters''' was completed. * '''1997 (Heisei 9)''' - Excavation of '''Kurozuka Kofun'''. * '''2014 (Heisei 26)''' - '''Cultural Property Storage and Research Center''' established in the former '''Nara Prefectural Murō High School'''. * '''2015 (Heisei 27)''' - The institute was transferred from the '''Nara Prefectural Board of Education''' to the '''Governor’s Office of Nara Prefecture'''. == Organization == * The '''Director-General''' oversees the '''Deputy Director''' and '''Museum Director'''. * The '''Deputy Director''' supervises: * '''Planning Division''' (企画部) * '''Planning Section''' (企画課) * '''Documentation Section''' (資料課) * '''Excavation Division''' (調査部) * '''Excavation Section''' (調査課) * '''General Affairs Section''' (総務課) * The '''Museum Director''' supervises the '''Deputy Museum Director''' and the '''Curatorial Section''' (学芸課). == Affiliated Museum == {{博物館 |名称 = Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum |image = Kashiharakouen-koukogaku02.jpg|Affiliated Museum |imagesize = 250 |正式名称 = 奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館 |専門分野 = {{ill|Archaeology|ja|考古学}}・{{ill|Buried Cultural Properties|ja|埋蔵文化財}} |所在地 = [[Nara Prefecture]] [[Kashihara, Nara]] |アクセス = [[Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu]] {{ill|Kintetsu Kashihara Line|ja|近鉄橿原線}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Yoshino Line|ja|近鉄吉野線}} - [[Kashiharajingū-mae Station]] (15-min walk)
[[Kintetsu Kashihara Line]] - [[Unebigoryōmae Station]] (5-min walk) |URL = [http://www.kashikoken.jp/museum/ Official Website] }} The '''museum houses approximately 3,700 artifacts''' spanning from the '''Paleolithic period to the Muromachi period'''.{{Cite web|和書|title=Nara's Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum Reopens|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211102110118/https://nordot.app/828174813890314240|website=Kyodo News|accessdate=2021-11-03|language=ja}} The museum was '''closed for renovations''' from '''December 24, 2018''', due to updates to the '''air conditioning system''' and concerns over the presence of '''harmful substances inside display cases'''. It '''reopened on November 3, 2021'''. == See Also == {{Commonscat|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture}} * {{ill|Category:People from Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|Category:奈良県立橿原考古学研究所の人物}} == External Links == * {{official|http://www.kashikoken.jp/}} * {{Facebook|kashikoken|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute}} [[Category:Completion tier 3]] {{pp-vandalism|small=yes}} [[file:Kashihara Archaeological Institute , Nara prefecture.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute]] The '''Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute''' (奈良県立橿原考古学研究所, *Nara Kenritsu Kashihara Kōkogaku Kenkyūjo*) is a '''prefectural institution''' located in {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市}}, specializing in the research and investigation of '''buried cultural properties''' (埋蔵文化財). It is an institution under the jurisdiction of '''Nara Prefecture'''. As one of the oldest '''public institutions for archaeological research''' still in operation today, it is sometimes abbreviated as '''Kashikōken''' (橿考研). The institute has conducted '''excavations of major historical sites''' within Nara Prefecture, including the '''Makimuku Ruins''' ({{ill|Makimuku ruins|ja|纒向遺跡}}), {{ill|Hokenoyama Kofun|ja|ホケノ山古墳}}, {{ill|Kurozuka Kofun|ja|黒塚古墳}}, {{ill|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun|ja|桜井茶臼山古墳}}, {{ill|Mesuriyama Kofun|ja|メスリ山古墳}}, {{ill|Fujinoki Kofun|ja|藤ノ木古墳}}, and {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}}. It has gained '''national recognition''' for its work on '''excavations of ancient capitals''' such as '''Asuka-kyō''' ({{ill|Asuka-dera|ja|飛鳥京跡}}), '''Tōdai-ji''' ({{ill|Tōdai-ji|ja|東大寺}}), and '''Tōshōdai-ji''' ({{ill|Tōshōdai-ji|ja|唐招提寺}}). The institute also manages an '''affiliated museum''' and operates a '''cultural property storage and research center''' in {{ill|Uda, Nara|ja|宇陀市}}. == History == * '''1938 (Shōwa 13)''' - Archaeological surveys of '''Kashihara Shrine’s outer precincts''' were conducted as part of the '''2600th Anniversary of Japan’s Founding Project''' (紀元二千六百年記念行事). '''Masao Suenaga''' ({{ill|Masao Suenaga|ja|末永雅雄}}) from '''Kyoto University''' led the survey on '''September 13''', which later became the institute’s '''founding date'''. * '''1951 (Shōwa 26)''' - The '''Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute''' was officially established with '''Masao Suenaga''' as the first director. * '''1972 (Shōwa 47)''' - The '''Takamatsuzuka Kofun murals''' were discovered during an excavation by the institute. * '''1974 (Shōwa 49)''' - The former '''Kashihara Kōen Archaeological Museum''' became an '''affiliated facility''', later renamed '''Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum'''. * '''1985 (Shōwa 60)''' - First excavation of '''Fujinoki Kofun'''. * '''1990 (Heisei 2)''' - The institute’s '''new headquarters''' was completed. * '''1997 (Heisei 9)''' - Excavation of '''Kurozuka Kofun'''. * '''2014 (Heisei 26)''' - '''Cultural Property Storage and Research Center''' established in the former '''Nara Prefectural Murō High School'''. * '''2015 (Heisei 27)''' - The institute was transferred from the '''Nara Prefectural Board of Education''' to the '''Governor’s Office of Nara Prefecture'''. == Organization == * The '''Director-General''' oversees the '''Deputy Director''' and '''Museum Director'''. * The '''Deputy Director''' supervises: * '''Planning Division''' (企画部) * '''Planning Section''' (企画課) * '''Documentation Section''' (資料課) * '''Excavation Division''' (調査部) * '''Excavation Section''' (調査課) * '''General Affairs Section''' (総務課) * The '''Museum Director''' supervises the '''Deputy Museum Director''' and the '''Curatorial Section''' (学芸課). == Affiliated Museum == {{博物館 |名称 = Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum |image = Kashiharakouen-koukogaku02.jpg|Affiliated Museum |imagesize = 250 |正式名称 = 奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館 |専門分野 = {{ill|Archaeology|ja|考古学}}・{{ill|Buried Cultural Properties|ja|埋蔵文化財}} |所在地 = [[Nara Prefecture]] [[Kashihara, Nara]] |アクセス = [[Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu]] {{ill|Kintetsu Kashihara Line|ja|近鉄橿原線}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Yoshino Line|ja|近鉄吉野線}} - [[Kashiharajingū-mae Station]] (15-min walk)
[[Kintetsu Kashihara Line]] - [[Unebigoryōmae Station]] (5-min walk) |URL = [http://www.kashikoken.jp/museum/ Official Website] }} The '''museum houses approximately 3,700 artifacts''' spanning from the '''Paleolithic period to the Muromachi period'''.{{Cite web|和書|title=Nara's Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum Reopens|url=https://web.archive.org/web/202qq102qq0qq8/https://nordot.app/828174813890314240|website=Kyodo News|accessdate=2021-qq-03|language=ja}} The museum was '''closed for renovations''' from '''December 24, 2018''', due to updates to the '''air conditioning system''' and concerns over the presence of '''harmful substances inside display cases'''. It '''reopened on November 3, 2021'''. == See Also == {{Commonscat|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture}} * {{ill|Category:People from Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|Category:奈良県立橿原考古学研究所の人物}} == External Links == * {{official|http://www.kashikoken.jp/}} * {{Facebook|kashikoken|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute}} [[Category:Completion tier 3]] {{pp-vandalism|small=yes}} [[file:Kashihara Archaeological Institute , Nara prefecture.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute]] The '''Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute''' (奈良県立橿原考古学研究所, *Nara Kenritsu Kashihara Kōkogaku Kenkyūjo*) is a '''prefectural institution''' located in {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市}}, specializing in the research and investigation of '''buried cultural properties''' (埋蔵文化財). It is an institution under the jurisdiction of '''Nara Prefecture'''. As one of the oldest '''public institutions for archaeological research''' still in operation today, it is sometimes abbreviated as '''Kashikōken''' (橿考研). The institute has conducted '''excavations of major historical sites''' within Nara Prefecture, including the '''Makimuku Ruins''' ({{ill|Makimuku ruins|ja|纒向遺跡}}), {{ill|Hokenoyama Kofun|ja|ホケノ山古墳}}, {{ill|Kurozuka Kofun|ja|黒塚古墳}}, {{ill|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun|ja|桜井茶臼山古墳}}, {{ill|Mesuriyama Kofun|ja|メスリ山古墳}}, {{ill|Fujinoki Kofun|ja|藤ノ木古墳}}, and {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}}. It has gained '''national recognition''' for its work on '''excavations of ancient capitals''' such as '''Asuka-kyō''' ({{ill|Asuka-dera|ja|飛鳥京跡}}), '''Tōdai-ji''' ({{ill|Tōdai-ji|ja|東大寺}}), and '''Tōshōdai-ji''' ({{ill|Tōshōdai-ji|ja|唐招提寺}}). The institute also manages an '''affiliated museum''' and operates a '''cultural property storage and research center''' in {{ill|Uda, Nara|ja|宇陀市}}. == History == * '''1938 (Shōwa 13)''' - Archaeological surveys of '''Kashihara Shrine’s outer precincts''' were conducted as part of the '''2600th Anniversary of Japan’s Founding Project''' (紀元二千六百年記念行事). '''Masao Suenaga''' ({{ill|Masao Suenaga|ja|末永雅雄}}) from '''Kyoto University''' led the survey on '''September 13''', which later became the institute’s '''founding date'''. * '''1951 (Shōwa 26)''' - The '''Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute''' was officially established with '''Masao Suenaga''' as the first director. * '''1972 (Shōwa 47)''' - The '''Takamatsuzuka Kofun murals''' were discovered during an excavation by the institute. * '''1974 (Shōwa 49)''' - The former '''Kashihara Kōen Archaeological Museum''' became an '''affiliated facility''', later renamed '''Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum'''. * '''1985 (Shōwa 60)''' - First excavation of '''Fujinoki Kofun'''. * '''1990 (Heisei 2)''' - The institute’s '''new headquarters''' was completed. * '''1997 (Heisei 9)''' - Excavation of '''Kurozuka Kofun'''. * '''2014 (Heisei 26)''' - '''Cultural Property Storage and Research Center''' established in the former '''Nara Prefectural Murō High School'''. * '''2015 (Heisei 27)''' - The institute was transferred from the '''Nara Prefectural Board of Education''' to the '''Governor’s Office of Nara Prefecture'''. == Organization == * The '''Director-General''' oversees the '''Deputy Director''' and '''Museum Director'''. * The '''Deputy Director''' supervises: * '''Planning Division''' (企画部) * '''Planning Section''' (企画課) * '''Documentation Section''' (資料課) * '''Excavation Division''' (調査部) * '''Excavation Section''' (調査課) * '''General Affairs Section''' (総務課) * The '''Museum Director''' supervises the '''Deputy Museum Director''' and the '''Curatorial Section''' (学芸課). == Affiliated Museum == {{博物館 |名称 = Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum |image = Kashiharakouen-koukogaku02.jpg|Affiliated Museum |imagesize = 250 |正式名称 = 奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館 |専門分野 = {{ill|Archaeology|ja|考古学}}・{{ill|Buried Cultural Properties|ja|埋蔵文化財}} |所在地 = [[Nara Prefecture]] [[Kashihara, Nara]] |アクセス = [[Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu]] {{ill|Kintetsu Kashihara Line|ja|近鉄橿原線}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}, {{ill|Kintetsu Yoshino Line|ja|近鉄吉野線}} - [[Kashiharajingū-mae Station]] (15-min walk)
[[Kintetsu Kashihara Line]] - [[Unebigoryōmae Station]] (5-min walk) |URL = [http://www.kashikoken.jp/museum/ Official Website] }} The '''museum houses approximately 3,700 artifacts''' spanning from the '''Paleolithic period to the Muromachi period'''.{{Cite web|和書|title=Nara's Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum Reopens|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211102110118/https://nordot.app/828174813890314240|website=Kyodo News|accessdate=2021-11-03|language=ja}} The museum was '''closed for renovations''' from '''December 24, 2018''', due to updates to the '''air conditioning system''' and concerns over the presence of '''harmful substances inside display cases'''. It '''reopened on November 3, 2021'''. == See Also == {{Commonscat|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture}} * {{ill|Category:People from Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|Category:奈良県立橿原考古学研究所の人物}} == External Links == * {{official|http://www.kashikoken.jp/}} * {{Facebook|kashikoken|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute}}
