[[co:commons:Category:Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture]][[de:Archäologisches Forschungsinstitut der Präfektur Nara in Kashihara]][[ja:奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]] [[co:commons:Category:Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture]][[de:Archäologisches Forschungsinstitut der Präfektur Nara in Kashihara]][[ja:奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]] [[co:commons:Category:Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture]][[de:Archäologisches Forschungsinstitut der Präfektur Nara in Kashihara]][[ja:奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]] [[sn:commons:Category:Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture]][[de:Archäologisches Forschungsinstitut der Präfektur Nara in Kashihara]][[ja:奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11445218]] Wikidata name: Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:17, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{pp-vandalism|small=yes}} The '''Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute''' (奈良県立橿原考古学研究所, *Nara Kenritsu Kashihara Kōkogaku Kenkyūjo*) is a '''prefectural institution''' located in [[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara, Nara]], specializing in the research and investigation of '''buried cultural properties''' (埋蔵文化財). It is an institution under the jurisdiction of '''Nara Prefecture'''. As one of the oldest '''public institutions for archaeological research''' still in operation today, it is sometimes abbreviated as '''Kashikōken''' (橿考研). The institute has conducted '''excavations of major historical sites''' within Nara Prefecture, including the '''Makimuku Ruins''' ([[:en:Makimuku ruins|Makimuku ruins]]), [[:en:Hokenoyama Kofun|Hokenoyama Kofun]], [[:en:Kurozuka Kofun|Kurozuka Kofun]], [[:en:Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun]], [[:en:Mesuriyama Kofun|Mesuriyama Kofun]], [[:en:Fujinoki Tomb|Fujinoki Kofun]], and [[:en:Takamatsuzuka Tomb|Takamatsuzuka Kofun]]. It has gained '''national recognition''' for its work on '''excavations of ancient capitals''' such as '''Asuka-kyō''' ({{ill|Asuka-dera|ja|飛鳥京跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka-dera|1=Asuka-kyō Ruins|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665955}}), '''Tōdai-ji''' ([[:en:Tōdai-ji|Tōdai-ji]]), and '''Tōshōdai-ji''' ([[:en:Tōshōdai-ji|Tōshōdai-ji]]). The institute also manages an '''affiliated museum''' and operates a '''cultural property storage and research center''' in [[:en:Uda, Nara|Uda, Nara]]. == History == * '''1938 (Shōwa 13)''' - Archaeological surveys of '''Kashihara Shrine’s outer precincts''' were conducted as part of the '''2600th Anniversary of Japan’s Founding Project''' (紀元二千六百年記念行事). '''Masao Suenaga''' ({{ill|Masao Suenaga|ja|末永雅雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masao Suenaga|1=Masao Suenaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11519783}}) from '''Kyoto University''' led the survey on '''September 13''', which later became the institute’s '''founding date'''. * '''1951 (Shōwa 26)''' - The '''Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute''' was officially established with '''Masao Suenaga''' as the first director. * '''1972 (Shōwa 47)''' - The '''Takamatsuzuka Kofun murals''' were discovered during an excavation by the institute. * '''1974 (Shōwa 49)''' - The former '''Kashihara Kōen Archaeological Museum''' became an '''affiliated facility''', later renamed '''Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum'''. * '''1985 (Shōwa 60)''' - First excavation of '''Fujinoki Kofun'''. * '''1990 (Heisei 2)''' - The institute’s '''new headquarters''' was completed. * '''1997 (Heisei 9)''' - Excavation of '''Kurozuka Kofun'''. * '''2014 (Heisei 26)''' * '''Cultural Property Storage and Research Center''' established in the former '''Nara Prefectural Murō High School'''. * '''2015 (Heisei 27)''' - The institute was transferred from the '''Nara Prefectural Board of Education''' to the '''Governor’s Office of Nara Prefecture'''. == Organization == * The '''Director-General''' oversees the '''Deputy Director''' and '''Museum Director'''. * The '''Deputy Director''' supervises: ** '''Planning Division''' (企画部) ** '''Planning Section''' (企画課) ** '''Documentation Section''' (資料課) ** '''Excavation Division''' (調査部) ** '''Excavation Section''' (調査課) ** '''General Affairs Section''' (総務課) * The '''Museum Director''' supervises the '''Deputy Museum Director''' and the '''Curatorial Section''' (学芸課). == Affiliated Museum == {{博物館 |名称 = Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum |image = Kashiharakouen-koukogaku02.jpg|Affiliated Museum |imagesize = 250 |正式名称 = 奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館 |専門分野 = [[:en:Archaeology|Archaeology]]・{{ill|Buried Cultural Properties|ja|埋蔵文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buried Cultural Properties|1=buried cultural property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11425912}} |所在地 = [[Nara Prefecture]] [[Kashihara, Nara]] |アクセス = [[Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu]] [[:en:Kashihara Line|Kintetsu Kashihara Line]], [[:en:Minami Osaka Line|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line]], [[:en:Yoshino Line|Kintetsu Yoshino Line]] - [[Kashiharajingū-mae Station]] (15-min walk)
[[Kintetsu Kashihara Line]] - [[Unebigoryōmae Station]] (5-min walk) |URL = [http://www.kashikoken.jp/museum/ Official Website] }} The '''museum houses approximately 3,700 artifacts''' spanning from the '''Paleolithic period to the Muromachi period'''.{{Cite web|和書|title=Nara's Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum Reopens|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211102110118/https://nordot.app/828174813890314240|website=Kyodo News|accessdate=2021-11-03|language=ja}} The museum was '''closed for renovations''' from '''December 24, 2018''', due to updates to the '''air conditioning system''' and concerns over the presence of '''harmful substances inside display cases'''. It '''reopened on November 3, 2021'''. == See Also == {{Commonscat|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture}} * == External Links == * {{official|http://www.kashikoken.jp/}} * {{Facebook|kashikoken|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute}} {{coord|region:JP|display=title}} {{translated page|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|version=104254781|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[file:Kashihara Archaeological Institute , Nara prefecture.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute]] [[de:Arch%C3%A4ologisches Forschungsinstitut der Pr%C3%A4fektur Nara in Kashihara]] ==Merged second translation== {{pp-vandalism|small=yes}} The '''Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute''' (奈良県立橿原考古学研究所, ''Nara Kenritsu Kashihara Kōkogaku Kenkyūsho'') is a prefectural research organization for archaeological excavations and studies, located in [[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is one of the oldest publicly operated archaeological institutes still in existence and is often abbreviated as '''Kashikōken''' (橿考研). The institute is renowned for its excavations of significant sites such as [[:en:Makimuku ruins|Makimuku Ruins]], [[:en:Hokenoyama Kofun|Hokenoyama Kofun]], [[:en:Kurozuka Kofun|Kurozuka Kofun]], and [[:en:Fujinoki Tomb|Fujinoki Tomb]], as well as historical locations like {{ill|Asuka Palace Ruins|ja|飛鳥京跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka Palace Ruins|1=Asuka-kyō Ruins|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665955}}, [[:en:Tōdai-ji|Tōdai-ji]], and [[:en:Tōshōdai-ji|Tōshōdai-ji]]. It also includes an attached museum and a facility in [[:en:Uda, Nara|Uda]] for the preservation and organization of cultural artifacts. == History == * 1938: The institute's origins trace back to the "2600th Anniversary of the Imperial Era" commemorative events, during which [[:en:Kashihara Shrine|Kashihara Shrine]] conducted an excavation of the {{ill|Kashihara Ruins|ja|橿原遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashihara Ruins|1=Kashihara Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24876647}} led by Masao Suenaga from Kyoto University. September 13, the start date of this excavation, is celebrated as the institute's founding date. * 1951: Officially established as the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute, with Masao Suenaga as its director. * 1972: Discovery of the mural paintings in [[:en:Takamatsuzuka Tomb|Takamatsuzuka Kofun]]. * 1974: The Archaeological Museum of Kashihara became an affiliated facility of the institute. * 1985: First excavation of the Fujinoki Kofun. * 1990: Completion tier 6]], {{ill|Cultural properties of Japan|ja|埋蔵文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cultural properties of Japan|1=buried cultural property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11425912}} | Location = [[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan | Website = [http://www.kashikoken.jp/museum/ Museum Official Site] }} The museum showcases approximately 3,700 artifacts from the Paleolithic through the Muromachi period.{{Cite web|和書|title=奈良の橿考研博物館リニューアル|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211102110118/https://nordot.app/828174813890314240|website=共同通信|accessdate=2021-11-03|language=ja}} Closed from December 24, 2018, for HVAC system updates, it reopened on November 3, 2021.[https://www.sankei.com/west/news/180417/wst1804170015-n1.html Museum closure announcement] {{lang|ja|産経ニュース}}, April 17, 2018. === Utilization Information === * Hours: 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM (last admission at 4:30 PM) * Closed: Mondays (or the following day if a holiday falls on a Monday), and from December 28 to January 4. * Admission Fees: ** Adults: ¥400 (¥350 for groups) ** Students (high school/university): ¥300 (¥250 for groups) ** Children (elementary/middle school): ¥200 (¥150 for groups) International tourists are admitted free of charge under Nara Prefecture's guidelines.{{Cite news|title=Why foreign tourists are free at Nara's museums while Japanese visitors pay?|newspaper=FNN|date=2024-03-07|url=https://www.fnn.jp/articles/-/667893|accessdate=2024-03-07}} === Highlights of Cultural Properties === * National Treasures and Important Cultural Properties include artifacts from significant kofun and archaeological sites in Nara Prefecture. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of personnel at the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological It is an institution under the jurisdiction of '''Nara Prefecture'''. As one of the oldest '''public institutions for archaeological research''' still in operation today, it is sometimes abbreviated as '''Kashikōken''' (橿考研). The institute has conducted '''excavations of major historical sites''' within Nara Prefecture, including the '''Makimuku Ruins''' ({{ill|Makimuku ruins|en|Makimuku ruins|WD=Q11608507|lt=Makimuku ruins}}), {{ill|Hokenoyama Kofun|en|Hokenoyama Kofun|WD=Q11337664|lt=Hokenoyama Kofun}}, {{ill|Kurozuka Kofun|en|Kurozuka Kofun|WD=Q1793171|lt=Kurozuka Kofun}}, {{ill|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun|en|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun|WD=Q11537882|lt=Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun}}, {{ill|Mesuriyama Kofun|en|Mesuriyama Kofun|WD=Q11343632|lt=Mesuriyama Kofun}}, {{ill|Fujinoki Tomb|en|Fujinoki Tomb|WD=Q3198273|lt=Fujinoki Kofun}}, and {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Tomb|en|Takamatsuzuka Tomb|WD=Q1039675|lt=Takamatsuzuka Kofun}}. It has gained '''national recognition''' for its work on '''excavations of ancient capitals''' such as '''Asuka-kyō''' ({{ill|Asuka-dera|ja|飛鳥京跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka-dera|1=Asuka-kyō Ruins|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665955}}), '''Tōdai-ji''' ({{ill|Tōdai-ji|en|Tōdai-ji|WD=Q460367|lt=Tōdai-ji}}), and '''Tōshōdai-ji''' ({{ill|Tōshōdai-ji|en|Tōshōdai-ji|WD=Q973175|lt=Tōshōdai-ji}}). The institute also manages an '''affiliated museum''' and operates a '''cultural property storage and research center''' in {{ill|Uda, Nara|en|Uda, Nara|WD=Q863747|lt=Uda, Nara}}. == History == * '''1938 (Shōwa 13)''' - Archaeological surveys of '''Kashihara Shrine’s outer precincts''' were conducted as part of the '''2600th Anniversary of Japan’s Founding Project''' (紀元二千六百年記念行事). '''Masao Suenaga''' ({{ill|Masao Suenaga|ja|末永雅雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masao Suenaga|1=Masao Suenaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11519783}}) from '''Kyoto University''' led the survey on '''September 13''', which later became the institute’s '''founding date'''. * '''1951 (Shōwa 26)''' - The '''Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute''' was officially established with '''Masao Suenaga''' as the first director. * '''1972 (Shōwa 47)''' - The '''Takamatsuzuka Kofun murals''' were discovered during an excavation by the institute. * '''1974 (Shōwa 49)''' - The former '''Kashihara Kōen Archaeological Museum''' became an '''affiliated facility''', later renamed '''Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum'''. * '''1985 (Shōwa 60)''' - First excavation of '''Fujinoki Kofun'''. * '''1990 (Heisei 2)''' - The institute’s '''new headquarters''' was completed. * '''1997 (Heisei 9)''' - Excavation of '''Kurozuka Kofun'''. * '''2014 (Heisei 26)''' * '''Cultural Property Storage and Research Center''' established in the former '''Nara Prefectural Murō High School'''. * '''2015 (Heisei 27)''' - The institute was transferred from the '''Nara Prefectural Board of Education''' to the '''Governor’s Office of Nara Prefecture'''. == Organization == * The '''Director-General''' oversees the '''Deputy Director''' and '''Museum Director'''. * The '''Deputy Director''' supervises: ** '''Planning Division''' (企画部) ** '''Planning Section''' (企画課) ** '''Documentation Section''' (資料課) ** '''Excavation Division''' (調査部) ** '''Excavation Section''' (調査課) ** '''General Affairs Section''' (総務課) * The '''Museum Director''' supervises the '''Deputy Museum Director''' and the '''Curatorial Section''' (学芸課). == Affiliated Museum == {{博物館 |名称 = Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum |image = Kashiharakouen-koukogaku02.jpg|Affiliated Museum |imagesize = 250 |正式名称 = 奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館 |専門分野 = {{ill|Archaeology|en|Archaeology|WD=Q23498|lt=Archaeology}}・{{ill|Buried Cultural Properties|ja|埋蔵文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buried Cultural Properties|1=buried cultural property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11425912}} |所在地 = [[Nara Prefecture]] [[Kashihara, Nara]] |アクセス = [[Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu]] {{ill|Kashihara Line|en|Kashihara Line|WD=Q2491066|lt=Kintetsu Kashihara Line}}, {{ill|Minami Osaka Line|en|Minami Osaka Line|WD=Q901342|lt=Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line}}, {{ill|Yoshino Line|en|Yoshino Line|WD=Q901085|lt=Kintetsu Yoshino Line}} - [[Kashiharajingū-mae Station]] (15-min walk)
[[Kintetsu Kashihara Line]] - [[Unebigoryōmae Station]] (5-min walk) |URL = [http://www.kashikoken.jp/museum/ Official Website] }} The '''museum houses approximately 3,700 artifacts''' spanning from the '''Paleolithic period to the Muromachi period'''.{{Cite web|和書|title=Nara's Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum Reopens|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211102110118/https://nordot.app/828174813890314240|website=Kyodo News|accessdate=2021-11-03|language=ja}} The museum was '''closed for renovations''' from '''December 24, 2018''', due to updates to the '''air conditioning system''' and concerns over the presence of '''harmful substances inside display cases'''. It '''reopened on November 3, 2021'''. == See Also == {{Commonscat|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture}} * == External Links == * {{official|http://www.kashikoken.jp/}} * {{Facebook|kashikoken|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute}} {{coord|region:JP|display=title}} {{translated page|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|version=104254781|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[file:Kashihara Archaeological Institute , Nara prefecture.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute]] [[de:Arch%C3%A4ologisches Forschungsinstitut der Pr%C3%A4fektur Nara in Kashihara]] ==Merged second translation== {{pp-vandalism|small=yes}} The '''Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute''' (奈良県立橿原考古学研究所, ''Nara Kenritsu Kashihara Kōkogaku Kenkyūsho'') is a prefectural research organization for archaeological excavations and studies, located in {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|en|Kashihara, Nara|WD=Q496319|lt=Kashihara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}, Japan. It is one of the oldest publicly operated archaeological institutes still in existence and is often abbreviated as '''Kashikōken''' (橿考研). The institute is renowned for its excavations of significant sites such as {{ill|Makimuku ruins|en|Makimuku ruins|WD=Q11608507|lt=Makimuku Ruins}}, {{ill|Hokenoyama Kofun|en|Hokenoyama Kofun|WD=Q11337664|lt=Hokenoyama Kofun}}, {{ill|Kurozuka Kofun|en|Kurozuka Kofun|WD=Q1793171|lt=Kurozuka Kofun}}, and {{ill|Fujinoki Tomb|en|Fujinoki Tomb|WD=Q3198273|lt=Fujinoki Tomb}}, as well as historical locations like {{ill|Asuka Palace Ruins|ja|飛鳥京跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka Palace Ruins|1=Asuka-kyō Ruins|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665955}}, {{ill|Tōdai-ji|en|Tōdai-ji|WD=Q460367|lt=Tōdai-ji}}, and {{ill|Tōshōdai-ji|en|Tōshōdai-ji|WD=Q973175|lt=Tōshōdai-ji}}. It also includes an attached museum and a facility in {{ill|Uda, Nara|en|Uda, Nara|WD=Q863747|lt=Uda}} for the preservation and organization of cultural artifacts. == History == * 1938: The institute's origins trace back to the "2600th Anniversary of the Imperial Era" commemorative events, during which {{ill|Kashihara Shrine|en|Kashihara Shrine|WD=Q710333|lt=Kashihara Shrine}} conducted an excavation of the {{ill|Kashihara Ruins|ja|橿原遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashihara Ruins|1=Kashihara Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24876647}} led by Masao Suenaga from Kyoto University. September 13, the start date of this excavation, is celebrated as the institute's founding date. * 1951: Officially established as the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute, with Masao Suenaga as its director. * 1972: Discovery of the mural paintings in {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Tomb|en|Takamatsuzuka Tomb|WD=Q1039675|lt=Takamatsuzuka Kofun}}. * 1974: The Archaeological Museum of Kashihara became an affiliated facility of the institute. * 1985: First excavation of the Fujinoki Kofun. * 1990: Completion tier 6]], {{ill|Cultural properties of Japan|ja|埋蔵文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cultural properties of Japan|1=buried cultural property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11425912}} | Location = {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|en|Kashihara, Nara|WD=Q496319|lt=Kashihara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}, Japan | Website = [http://www.kashikoken.jp/museum/ Museum Official Site] }} The museum showcases approximately 3,700 artifacts from the Paleolithic through the Muromachi period.{{Cite web|和書|title=奈良の橿考研博物館リニューアル|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211102110118/https://nordot.app/828174813890314240|website=共同通信|accessdate=2021-11-03|language=ja}} Closed from December 24, 2018, for HVAC system updates, it reopened on November 3, 2021.[https://www.sankei.com/west/news/180417/wst1804170015-n1.html Museum closure announcement] {{lang|ja|産経ニュース}}, April 17, 2018. === Utilization Information === * Hours: 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM (last admission at 4:30 PM) * Closed: Mondays (or the following day if a holiday falls on a Monday), and from December 28 to January 4. * Admission Fees: ** Adults: ¥400 (¥350 for groups) ** Students (high school/university): ¥300 (¥250 for groups) ** Children (elementary/middle school): ¥200 (¥150 for groups) International tourists are admitted free of charge under Nara Prefecture's guidelines.{{Cite news|title=Why foreign tourists are free at Nara's museums while Japanese visitors pay?|newspaper=FNN|date=2024-03-07|url=https://www.fnn.jp/articles/-/667893|accessdate=2024-03-07}} === Highlights of Cultural Properties === * National Treasures and Important Cultural Properties include artifacts from significant kofun and archaeological sites in Nara Prefecture. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of personnel at the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所の人物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of personnel at the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == External Links == * {{official|http://www.kashikoken.jp/}} * {{Facebook|kashikoken|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute}} {{coord|region:JP|display=title}} {{translated page|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|version=104254781|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[file:Kashihara Archaeological Institute , Nara prefecture.jpg|thumb|200px|right|The Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute]] [[de:Arch%C3%A4ologisches Forschungsinstitut der Pr%C3%A4fektur Nara in Kashihara]] [[ja:奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]] {{Commons category|Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture}} [[de:Archäologisches Forschungsinstitut der Präfektur Nara in Kashihara]] {{Commons category|Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture}} [[de:Archäologisches Forschungsinstitut der Präfektur Nara in Kashihara]] {{wikidata link|Q11445218}} [[Category: of the main building and other new facilities. * 1997: Excavation of the Kurozuka Kofun. * 2014: Establishment of the Uda Cultural Heritage Center at the site of the former Murō High School. * 2015: Transition from the Nara Prefectural Board of Education to the jurisdiction of the Governor of Nara Prefecture. == Organization == The institute is headed by a director, with departments including: * Deputy Director overseeing the Planning Division, Research Division, and General Affairs Section. * Attached Museum led by a museum director and a vice director, supervising the Curatorial Section. == Attached Museum == {{Museum | Name = Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Museum | revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture]][[de:Archäologisches Forschungsinstitut der Präfektur Nara in Kashihara]][[ja:奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]] [[co:commons:Category:Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture]][[de:Archäologisches Forschungsinstitut der Präfektur Nara in Kashihara]][[ja:奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]] [[co:commons:Category:Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture]][[de:Archäologisches Forschungsinstitut der Präfektur Nara in Kashihara]][[ja:奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]] [[sn:commons:Category:Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture]][[de:Archäologisches Forschungsinstitut der Präfektur Nara in Kashihara]][[ja:奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11445218]] Wikidata name: Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:17, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{pp-vandalism|small=yes}} The '''Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute''' (奈良県立橿原考古学研究所, *Nara Kenritsu Kashihara Kōkogaku Kenkyūjo*) is a '''prefectural institution''' located in {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|en|Kashihara, Nara|WD=Q496319|lt=Kashihara, Nara}}, specializing in the research and investigation of '''buried cultural properties''' (埋蔵文化財). It is an institution under the jurisdiction of '''Nara Prefecture'''. As one of the oldest '''public institutions for archaeological research''' still in operation today, it is sometimes abbreviated as '''Kashikōken''' (橿考研). The institute has conducted '''excavations of major historical sites''' within Nara Prefecture, including the '''Makimuku Ruins''' ({{ill|Makimuku ruins|en|Makimuku ruins|WD=Q11608507|lt=Makimuku ruins}}), {{ill|Hokenoyama Kofun|en|Hokenoyama Kofun|WD=Q11337664|lt=Hokenoyama Kofun}}, {{ill|Kurozuka Kofun|en|Kurozuka Kofun|WD=Q1793171|lt=Kurozuka Kofun}}, {{ill|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun|en|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun|WD=Q11537882|lt=Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun}}, {{ill|Mesuriyama Kofun|en|Mesuriyama Kofun|WD=Q11343632|lt=Mesuriyama Kofun}}, {{ill|Fujinoki Tomb|en|Fujinoki Tomb|WD=Q3198273|lt=Fujinoki Kofun}}, and {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Tomb|en|Takamatsuzuka Tomb|WD=Q1039675|lt=Takamatsuzuka Kofun}}. It has gained '''national recognition''' for its work on '''excavations of ancient capitals''' such as '''Asuka-kyō''' ({{ill|Asuka-dera|ja|飛鳥京跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka-dera|1=Asuka-kyō Ruins|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665955}}), '''Tōdai-ji''' ({{ill|Tōdai-ji|en|Tōdai-ji|WD=Q460367|lt=Tōdai-ji}}), and '''Tōshōdai-ji''' ({{ill|Tōshōdai-ji|en|Tōshōdai-ji|WD=Q973175|lt=Tōshōdai-ji}}). The institute also manages an '''affiliated museum''' and operates a '''cultural property storage and research center''' in {{ill|Uda, Nara|en|Uda, Nara|WD=Q863747|lt=Uda, Nara}}. == History == * '''1938 (Shōwa 13)''' - Archaeological surveys of '''Kashihara Shrine’s outer precincts''' were conducted as part of the '''2600th Anniversary of Japan’s Founding Project''' (紀元二千六百年記念行事). '''Masao Suenaga''' ({{ill|Masao Suenaga|ja|末永雅雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masao Suenaga|1=Masao Suenaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11519783}}) from '''Kyoto University''' led the survey on '''September 13''', which later became the institute’s '''founding date'''. * '''1951 (Shōwa 26)''' - The '''Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute''' was officially established with '''Masao Suenaga''' as the first director. * '''1972 (Shōwa 47)''' - The '''Takamatsuzuka Kofun murals''' were discovered during an excavation by the institute. * '''1974 (Shōwa 49)''' - The former '''Kashihara Kōen Archaeological Museum''' became an '''affiliated facility''', later renamed '''Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum'''. * '''1985 (Shōwa 60)''' - First excavation of '''Fujinoki Kofun'''. * '''1990 (Heisei 2)''' - The institute’s '''new headquarters''' was completed. * '''1997 (Heisei 9)''' - Excavation of '''Kurozuka Kofun'''. * '''2014 (Heisei 26)''' * '''Cultural Property Storage and Research Center''' established in the former '''Nara Prefectural Murō High School'''. * '''2015 (Heisei 27)''' - The institute was transferred from the '''Nara Prefectural Board of Education''' to the '''Governor’s Office of Nara Prefecture'''. == Organization == * The '''Director-General''' oversees the '''Deputy Director''' and '''Museum Director'''. * The '''Deputy Director''' supervises: ** '''Planning Division''' (企画部) ** '''Planning Section''' (企画課) ** '''Documentation Section''' (資料課) ** '''Excavation Division''' (調査部) ** '''Excavation Section''' (調査課) ** '''General Affairs Section''' (総務課) * The '''Museum Director''' supervises the '''Deputy Museum Director''' and the '''Curatorial Section''' (学芸課). == Affiliated Museum == {{博物館 |名称 = Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum |image = Kashiharakouen-koukogaku02.jpg|Affiliated Museum |imagesize = 250 |正式名称 = 奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館 |専門分野 = {{ill|Archaeology|en|Archaeology|WD=Q23498|lt=Archaeology}}・{{ill|Buried Cultural Properties|ja|埋蔵文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buried Cultural Properties|1=buried cultural property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11425912}} |所在地 = [[Nara Prefecture]] [[Kashihara, Nara]] |アクセス = [[Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu]] {{ill|Kashihara Line|en|Kashihara Line|WD=Q2491066|lt=Kintetsu Kashihara Line}}, {{ill|Minami Osaka Line|en|Minami Osaka Line|WD=Q901342|lt=Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line}}, {{ill|Yoshino Line|en|Yoshino Line|WD=Q901085|lt=Kintetsu Yoshino Line}} - [[Kashiharajingū-mae Station]] (15-min walk)
[[Kintetsu Kashihara Line]] - [[Unebigoryōmae Station]] (5-min walk) |URL = [http://www.kashikoken.jp/museum/ Official Website] }} The '''museum houses approximately 3,700 artifacts''' spanning from the '''Paleolithic period to the Muromachi period'''.{{Cite web|和書|title=Nara's Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum Reopens|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211102110118/https://nordot.app/828174813890314240|website=Kyodo News|accessdate=2021-11-03|language=ja}} The museum was '''closed for renovations''' from '''December 24, 2018''', due to updates to the '''air conditioning system''' and concerns over the presence of '''harmful substances inside display cases'''. It '''reopened on November 3, 2021'''. == See Also == {{Commonscat|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture}} * == External Links == * {{official|http://www.kashikoken.jp/}} * {{Facebook|kashikoken|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute}} {{coord|region:JP|display=title}} {{translated page|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|version=104254781|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[file:Kashihara Archaeological Institute , Nara prefecture.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute]] [[de:Arch%C3%A4ologisches Forschungsinstitut der Pr%C3%A4fektur Nara in Kashihara]] ==Merged second translation== {{pp-vandalism|small=yes}} The '''Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute''' (奈良県立橿原考古学研究所, ''Nara Kenritsu Kashihara Kōkogaku Kenkyūsho'') is a prefectural research organization for archaeological excavations and studies, located in {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|en|Kashihara, Nara|WD=Q496319|lt=Kashihara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}, Japan. It is one of the oldest publicly operated archaeological institutes still in existence and is often abbreviated as '''Kashikōken''' (橿考研). The institute is renowned for its excavations of significant sites such as {{ill|Makimuku ruins|en|Makimuku ruins|WD=Q11608507|lt=Makimuku Ruins}}, {{ill|Hokenoyama Kofun|en|Hokenoyama Kofun|WD=Q11337664|lt=Hokenoyama Kofun}}, {{ill|Kurozuka Kofun|en|Kurozuka Kofun|WD=Q1793171|lt=Kurozuka Kofun}}, and {{ill|Fujinoki Tomb|en|Fujinoki Tomb|WD=Q3198273|lt=Fujinoki Tomb}}, as well as historical locations like {{ill|Asuka Palace Ruins|ja|飛鳥京跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka Palace Ruins|1=Asuka-kyō Ruins|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665955}}, {{ill|Tōdai-ji|en|Tōdai-ji|WD=Q460367|lt=Tōdai-ji}}, and {{ill|Tōshōdai-ji|en|Tōshōdai-ji|WD=Q973175|lt=Tōshōdai-ji}}. It also includes an attached museum and a facility in {{ill|Uda, Nara|en|Uda, Nara|WD=Q863747|lt=Uda}} for the preservation and organization of cultural artifacts. == History == * 1938: The institute's origins trace back to the "2600th Anniversary of the Imperial Era" commemorative events, during which {{ill|Kashihara Shrine|en|Kashihara Shrine|WD=Q710333|lt=Kashihara Shrine}} conducted an excavation of the {{ill|Kashihara Ruins|ja|橿原遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashihara Ruins|1=Kashihara Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24876647}} led by Masao Suenaga from Kyoto University. September 13, the start date of this excavation, is celebrated as the institute's founding date. * 1951: Officially established as the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute, with Masao Suenaga as its director. * 1972: Discovery of the mural paintings in {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Tomb|en|Takamatsuzuka Tomb|WD=Q1039675|lt=Takamatsuzuka Kofun}}. * 1974: The Archaeological Museum of Kashihara became an affiliated facility of the institute. * 1985: First excavation of the Fujinoki Kofun. * 1990: Completion tier 6]], {{ill|Cultural properties of [[co:commons:Category:Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture]][[de:Archäologisches Forschungsinstitut der Präfektur Nara in Kashihara]][[ja:奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]] [[co:commons:Category:Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture]][[de:Archäologisches Forschungsinstitut der Präfektur Nara in Kashihara]][[ja:奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]] [[co:commons:Category:Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture]][[de:Archäologisches Forschungsinstitut der Präfektur Nara in Kashihara]][[ja:奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]] [[sn:commons:Category:Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture]][[de:Archäologisches Forschungsinstitut der Präfektur Nara in Kashihara]][[ja:奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]] {{pp-vandalism|small=yes}} The '''Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute''' (奈良県立橿原考古学研究所, *Nara Kenritsu Kashihara Kōkogaku Kenkyūjo*) is a '''prefectural institution''' located in {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|en|Kashihara, Nara|WD=Q496319|lt=Kashihara, Nara}}, specializing in the research and investigation of '''buried cultural properties''' (埋蔵文化財). It is an institution under the jurisdiction of '''Nara Prefecture'''. As one of the oldest '''public institutions for archaeological research''' still in operation today, it is sometimes abbreviated as '''Kashikōken''' (橿考研). The institute has conducted '''excavations of major historical sites''' within Nara Prefecture, including the '''Makimuku Ruins''' ({{ill|Makimuku ruins|en|Makimuku ruins|WD=Q11608507|lt=Makimuku ruins}}), {{ill|Hokenoyama Kofun|en|Hokenoyama Kofun|WD=Q11337664|lt=Hokenoyama Kofun}}, {{ill|Kurozuka Kofun|en|Kurozuka Kofun|WD=Q1793171|lt=Kurozuka Kofun}}, {{ill|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun|en|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun|WD=Q11537882|lt=Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun}}, {{ill|Mesuriyama Kofun|en|Mesuriyama Kofun|WD=Q11343632|lt=Mesuriyama Kofun}}, {{ill|Fujinoki Tomb|en|Fujinoki Tomb|WD=Q3198273|lt=Fujinoki Kofun}}, and {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Tomb|en|Takamatsuzuka Tomb|WD=Q1039675|lt=Takamatsuzuka Kofun}}. It has gained '''national recognition''' for its work on '''excavations of ancient capitals''' such as '''Asuka-kyō''' ({{ill|Asuka-dera|ja|飛鳥京跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka-dera|1=Asuka-kyō Ruins|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665955}}), '''Tōdai-ji''' ({{ill|Tōdai-ji|en|Tōdai-ji|WD=Q460367|lt=Tōdai-ji}}), and '''Tōshōdai-ji''' ({{ill|Tōshōdai-ji|en|Tōshōdai-ji|WD=Q973175|lt=Tōshōdai-ji}}). The institute also manages an '''affiliated museum''' and operates a '''cultural property storage and research center''' in {{ill|Uda, Nara|en|Uda, Nara|WD=Q863747|lt=Uda, Nara}}. == History == * '''1938 (Shōwa 13)''' - Archaeological surveys of '''Kashihara Shrine’s outer precincts''' were conducted as part of the '''2600th Anniversary of Japan’s Founding Project''' (紀元二千六百年記念行事). '''Masao Suenaga''' ({{ill|Masao Suenaga|ja|末永雅雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masao Suenaga|1=Masao Suenaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11519783}}) from '''Kyoto University''' led the survey on '''September 13''', which later became the institute’s '''founding date'''. * '''1951 (Shōwa 26)''' - The '''Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute''' was officially established with '''Masao Suenaga''' as the first director. * '''1972 (Shōwa 47)''' - The '''Takamatsuzuka Kofun murals''' were discovered during an excavation by the institute. * '''1974 (Shōwa 49)''' - The former '''Kashihara Kōen Archaeological Museum''' became an '''affiliated facility''', later renamed '''Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum'''. * '''1985 (Shōwa 60)''' - First excavation of '''Fujinoki Kofun'''. * '''1990 (Heisei 2)''' - The institute’s '''new headquarters''' was completed. * '''1997 (Heisei 9)''' - Excavation of '''Kurozuka Kofun'''. * '''2014 (Heisei 26)''' * '''Cultural Property Storage and Research Center''' established in the former '''Nara Prefectural Murō High School'''. * '''2015 (Heisei 27)''' - The institute was transferred from the '''Nara Prefectural Board of Education''' to the '''Governor’s Office of Nara Prefecture'''. == Organization == * The '''Director-General''' oversees the '''Deputy Director''' and '''Museum Director'''. * The '''Deputy Director''' supervises: ** '''Planning Division''' (企画部) ** '''Planning Section''' (企画課) ** '''Documentation Section''' (資料課) ** '''Excavation Division''' (調査部) ** '''Excavation Section''' (調査課) ** '''General Affairs Section''' (総務課) * The '''Museum Director''' supervises the '''Deputy Museum Director''' and the '''Curatorial Section''' (学芸課). == Affiliated Museum == {{博物館 |名称 = Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum |image = Kashiharakouen-koukogaku02.jpg|Affiliated Museum |imagesize = 250 |正式名称 = 奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館 |専門分野 = {{ill|Archaeology|en|Archaeology|WD=Q23498|lt=Archaeology}}・{{ill|Buried Cultural Properties|ja|埋蔵文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buried Cultural Properties|1=buried cultural property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11425912}} |所在地 = [[Nara Prefecture]] [[Kashihara, Nara]] |アクセス = [[Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu]] {{ill|Kashihara Line|en|Kashihara Line|WD=Q2491066|lt=Kintetsu Kashihara Line}}, {{ill|Minami Osaka Line|en|Minami Osaka Line|WD=Q901342|lt=Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line}}, {{ill|Yoshino Line|en|Yoshino Line|WD=Q901085|lt=Kintetsu Yoshino Line}} - [[Kashiharajingū-mae Station]] (15-min walk)
[[Kintetsu Kashihara Line]] - [[Unebigoryōmae Station]] (5-min walk) |URL = [http://www.kashikoken.jp/museum/ Official Website] }} The '''museum houses approximately 3,700 artifacts''' spanning from the '''Paleolithic period to the Muromachi period'''.{{Cite web|和書|title=Nara's Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum Reopens|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211102110118/https://nordot.app/828174813890314240|website=Kyodo News|accessdate=2021-11-03|language=ja}} The museum was '''closed for renovations''' from '''December 24, 2018''', due to updates to the '''air conditioning system''' and concerns over the presence of '''harmful substances inside display cases'''. It '''reopened on November 3, 2021'''. == See Also == {{Commonscat|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture}} * == External Links == * {{official|http://www.kashikoken.jp/}} * {{Facebook|kashikoken|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute}} {{coord|region:JP|display=title}} {{translated page|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|version=104254781|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[file:Kashihara Archaeological Institute , Nara prefecture.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute]] [[de:Arch%C3%A4ologisches Forschungsinstitut der Pr%C3%A4fektur Nara in Kashihara]] ==Merged second translation== {{pp-vandalism|small=yes}} The '''Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute''' (奈良県立橿原考古学研究所, ''Nara Kenritsu Kashihara Kōkogaku Kenkyūsho'') is a prefectural research organization for archaeological excavations and studies, located in {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|en|Kashihara, Nara|WD=Q496319|lt=Kashihara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}, Japan. It is one of the oldest publicly operated archaeological institutes still in existence and is often abbreviated as '''Kashikōken''' (橿考研). The institute is renowned for its excavations of significant sites such as {{ill|Makimuku ruins|en|Makimuku ruins|WD=Q11608507|lt=Makimuku Ruins}}, {{ill|Hokenoyama Kofun|en|Hokenoyama Kofun|WD=Q11337664|lt=Hokenoyama Kofun}}, {{ill|Kurozuka Kofun|en|Kurozuka Kofun|WD=Q1793171|lt=Kurozuka Kofun}}, and {{ill|Fujinoki Tomb|en|Fujinoki Tomb|WD=Q3198273|lt=Fujinoki Tomb}}, as well as historical locations like {{ill|Asuka Palace Ruins|ja|飛鳥京跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka Palace Ruins|1=Asuka-kyō Ruins|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665955}}, {{ill|Tōdai-ji|en|Tōdai-ji|WD=Q460367|lt=Tōdai-ji}}, and {{ill|Tōshōdai-ji|en|Tōshōdai-ji|WD=Q973175|lt=Tōshōdai-ji}}. It also includes an attached museum and a facility in {{ill|Uda, Nara|en|Uda, Nara|WD=Q863747|lt=Uda}} for the preservation and organization of cultural artifacts. == History == * 1938: The institute's origins trace back to the "2600th Anniversary of the Imperial Era" commemorative events, during which {{ill|Kashihara Shrine|en|Kashihara Shrine|WD=Q710333|lt=Kashihara Shrine}} conducted an excavation of the {{ill|Kashihara Ruins|ja|橿原遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashihara Ruins|1=Kashihara Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24876647}} led by Masao Suenaga from Kyoto University. September 13, the start date of this excavation, is celebrated as the institute's founding date. * 1951: Officially established as the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute, with Masao Suenaga as its director. * 1972: Discovery of the mural paintings in {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Tomb|en|Takamatsuzuka Tomb|WD=Q1039675|lt=Takamatsuzuka Kofun}}. * 1974: The Archaeological Museum of Kashihara became an affiliated facility of the institute. * 1985: First excavation of the Fujinoki Kofun. * 1990: Completion tier 6]], {{ill|Cultural properties of Japan|ja|埋蔵文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cultural properties of Japan|1=buried cultural property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11425912}} | Location = {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|en|Kashihara, Nara|WD=Q496319|lt=Kashihara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}, Japan | Website = [http://www.kashikoken.jp/museum/ Museum Official Site] }} The museum showcases approximately 3,700 artifacts from the Paleolithic through the Muromachi period.{{Cite web|和書|title=奈良の橿考研博物館リニューアル|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211102110118/https://nordot.app/828174813890314240|website=共同通信|accessdate=2021-11-03|language=ja}} Closed from December 24, 2018, for HVAC system updates, it reopened on November 3, 2021.[https://www.sankei.com/west/news/180417/wst1804170015-n1.html Museum closure announcement] {{lang|ja|産経ニュース}}, April 17, 2018. === Utilization Information === * Hours: 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM (last admission at 4:30 PM) * Closed: Mondays (or the following day if a holiday falls on a Monday), and from December 28 to January 4. * Admission Fees: ** Adults: ¥400 (¥350 for groups) ** Students (high school/university): ¥300 (¥250 for groups) ** Children (elementary/middle school): ¥200 (¥150 for groups) International tourists are admitted free of charge under Nara Prefecture's guidelines.{{Cite news|title=Why foreign tourists are free at Nara's museums while Japanese visitors pay?|newspaper=FNN|date=2024-03-07|url=https://www.fnn.jp/articles/-/667893|accessdate=2024-03-07}} === Highlights of Cultural Properties === * National Treasures and Important Cultural Properties include artifacts from significant kofun and archaeological sites in Nara Prefecture. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of personnel at the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所の人物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of personnel at the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == External Links == * {{official|http://www.kashikoken.jp/}} * {{Facebook|kashikoken|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute}} {{coord|region:JP|display=title}} {{translated page|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|version=104254781|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[file:Kashihara Archaeological Institute , Nara prefecture.jpg|thumb|200px|right|The Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute]] [[de:Arch%C3%A4ologisches Forschungsinstitut der Pr%C3%A4fektur Nara in Kashihara]] [[ja:奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]] {{Commons category|Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture}} [[de:Archäologisches Forschungsinstitut der Präfektur Nara in Kashihara]] {{Commons category|Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture}} [[de:Archäologisches Forschungsinstitut der Präfektur Nara in Kashihara]] {{wikidata link|Q11445218}} [[Category: of the main building and other new facilities. * 1997: Excavation of the Kurozuka Kofun. * 2014: Establishment of the Uda Cultural Heritage Center at the site of the former Murō High School. * 2015: Transition from the Nara Prefectural Board of Education to the jurisdiction of the Governor of Nara Prefecture. == Organization == The institute is headed by a director, with departments including: * Deputy Director overseeing the Planning Division, Research Division, and General Affairs Section. * Attached Museum led by a museum director and a vice director, supervising the Curatorial Section. == Attached Museum == {{Museum | Name = Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Museum | It is an institution under the jurisdiction of '''Nara Prefecture'''. As one of the oldest '''public institutions for archaeological research''' still in operation today, it is sometimes abbreviated as '''Kashikōken''' (橿考研). The institute has conducted '''excavations of major historical sites''' within Nara Prefecture, including the '''Makimuku Ruins''' ({{ill|Makimuku ruins|en|Makimuku ruins|WD=Q11608507|lt=Makimuku ruins}}), {{ill|Hokenoyama Kofun|en|Hokenoyama Kofun|WD=Q11337664|lt=Hokenoyama Kofun}}, {{ill|Kurozuka Kofun|en|Kurozuka Kofun|WD=Q1793171|lt=Kurozuka Kofun}}, {{ill|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun|en|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun|WD=Q11537882|lt=Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun}}, {{ill|Mesuriyama Kofun|en|Mesuriyama Kofun|WD=Q11343632|lt=Mesuriyama Kofun}}, {{ill|Fujinoki Tomb|en|Fujinoki Tomb|WD=Q3198273|lt=Fujinoki Kofun}}, and {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Tomb|en|Takamatsuzuka Tomb|WD=Q1039675|lt=Takamatsuzuka Kofun}}. It has gained '''national recognition''' for its work on '''excavations of ancient capitals''' such as '''Asuka-kyō''' ({{ill|Asuka-dera|ja|飛鳥京跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka-dera|1=Asuka-kyō Ruins|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665955}}), '''Tōdai-ji''' ({{ill|Tōdai-ji|en|Tōdai-ji|WD=Q460367|lt=Tōdai-ji}}), and '''Tōshōdai-ji''' ({{ill|Tōshōdai-ji|en|Tōshōdai-ji|WD=Q973175|lt=Tōshōdai-ji}}). The institute also manages an '''affiliated museum''' and operates a '''cultural property storage and research center''' in {{ill|Uda, Nara|en|Uda, Nara|WD=Q863747|lt=Uda, Nara}}. == History == * '''1938 (Shōwa 13)''' - Archaeological surveys of '''Kashihara Shrine’s outer precincts''' were conducted as part of the '''2600th Anniversary of Japan’s Founding Project''' (紀元二千六百年記念行事). '''Masao Suenaga''' ({{ill|Masao Suenaga|ja|末永雅雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masao Suenaga|1=Masao Suenaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11519783}}) from '''Kyoto University''' led the survey on '''September 13''', which later became the institute’s '''founding date'''. * '''1951 (Shōwa 26)''' - The '''Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute''' was officially established with '''Masao Suenaga''' as the first director. * '''1972 (Shōwa 47)''' - The '''Takamatsuzuka Kofun murals''' were discovered during an excavation by the institute. * '''1974 (Shōwa 49)''' - The former '''Kashihara Kōen Archaeological Museum''' became an '''affiliated facility''', later renamed '''Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum'''. * '''1985 (Shōwa 60)''' - First excavation of '''Fujinoki Kofun'''. * '''1990 (Heisei 2)''' - The institute’s '''new headquarters''' was completed. * '''1997 (Heisei 9)''' - Excavation of '''Kurozuka Kofun'''. * '''2014 (Heisei 26)''' * '''Cultural Property Storage and Research Center''' established in the former '''Nara Prefectural Murō High School'''. * '''2015 (Heisei 27)''' - The institute was transferred from the '''Nara Prefectural Board of Education''' to the '''Governor’s Office of Nara Prefecture'''. == Organization == * The '''Director-General''' oversees the '''Deputy Director''' and '''Museum Director'''. * The '''Deputy Director''' supervises: ** '''Planning Division''' (企画部) ** '''Planning Section''' (企画課) ** '''Documentation Section''' (資料課) ** '''Excavation Division''' (調査部) ** '''Excavation Section''' (調査課) ** '''General Affairs Section''' (総務課) * The '''Museum Director''' supervises the '''Deputy Museum Director''' and the '''Curatorial Section''' (学芸課). == Affiliated Museum == {{博物館 |名称 = Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum |image = Kashiharakouen-koukogaku02.jpg|Affiliated Museum |imagesize = 250 |正式名称 = 奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館 |専門分野 = {{ill|Archaeology|en|Archaeology|WD=Q23498|lt=Archaeology}}・{{ill|Buried Cultural Properties|ja|埋蔵文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buried Cultural Properties|1=buried cultural property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11425912}} |所在地 = [[Nara Prefecture]] [[Kashihara, Nara]] |アクセス = [[Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu]] {{ill|Kashihara Line|en|Kashihara Line|WD=Q2491066|lt=Kintetsu Kashihara Line}}, {{ill|Minami Osaka Line|en|Minami Osaka Line|WD=Q901342|lt=Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line}}, {{ill|Yoshino Line|en|Yoshino Line|WD=Q901085|lt=Kintetsu Yoshino Line}} - [[Kashiharajingū-mae Station]] (15-min walk)
[[Kintetsu Kashihara Line]] - [[Unebigoryōmae Station]] (5-min walk) |URL = [http://www.kashikoken.jp/museum/ Official Website] }} The '''museum houses approximately 3,700 artifacts''' spanning from the '''Paleolithic period to the Muromachi period'''.{{Cite web|和書|title=Nara's Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum Reopens|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211102110118/https://nordot.app/828174813890314240|website=Kyodo News|accessdate=2021-11-03|language=ja}} The museum was '''closed for renovations''' from '''December 24, 2018''', due to updates to the '''air conditioning system''' and concerns over the presence of '''harmful substances inside display cases'''. It '''reopened on November 3, 2021'''. == See Also == {{Commonscat|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture}} * == External Links == * {{official|http://www.kashikoken.jp/}} * {{Facebook|kashikoken|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute}} {{coord|region:JP|display=title}} {{translated page|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|version=104254781|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[file:Kashihara Archaeological Institute , Nara prefecture.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute]] [[de:Arch%C3%A4ologisches Forschungsinstitut der Pr%C3%A4fektur Nara in Kashihara]] ==Merged second translation== {{pp-vandalism|small=yes}} The '''Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute''' (奈良県立橿原考古学研究所, ''Nara Kenritsu Kashihara Kōkogaku Kenkyūsho'') is a prefectural research organization for archaeological excavations and studies, located in {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|en|Kashihara, Nara|WD=Q496319|lt=Kashihara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}, Japan. It is one of the oldest publicly operated archaeological institutes still in existence and is often abbreviated as '''Kashikōken''' (橿考研). The institute is renowned for its excavations of significant sites such as {{ill|Makimuku ruins|en|Makimuku ruins|WD=Q11608507|lt=Makimuku Ruins}}, {{ill|Hokenoyama Kofun|en|Hokenoyama Kofun|WD=Q11337664|lt=Hokenoyama Kofun}}, {{ill|Kurozuka Kofun|en|Kurozuka Kofun|WD=Q1793171|lt=Kurozuka Kofun}}, and {{ill|Fujinoki Tomb|en|Fujinoki Tomb|WD=Q3198273|lt=Fujinoki Tomb}}, as well as historical locations like {{ill|Asuka Palace Ruins|ja|飛鳥京跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka Palace Ruins|1=Asuka-kyō Ruins|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665955}}, {{ill|Tōdai-ji|en|Tōdai-ji|WD=Q460367|lt=Tōdai-ji}}, and {{ill|Tōshōdai-ji|en|Tōshōdai-ji|WD=Q973175|lt=Tōshōdai-ji}}. It also includes an attached museum and a facility in {{ill|Uda, Nara|en|Uda, Nara|WD=Q863747|lt=Uda}} for the preservation and organization of cultural artifacts. == History == * 1938: The institute's origins trace back to the "2600th Anniversary of the Imperial Era" commemorative events, during which {{ill|Kashihara Shrine|en|Kashihara Shrine|WD=Q710333|lt=Kashihara Shrine}} conducted an excavation of the {{ill|Kashihara Ruins|ja|橿原遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashihara Ruins|1=Kashihara Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24876647}} led by Masao Suenaga from Kyoto University. September 13, the start date of this excavation, is celebrated as the institute's founding date. * 1951: Officially established as the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute, with Masao Suenaga as its director. * 1972: Discovery of the mural paintings in {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Tomb|en|Takamatsuzuka Tomb|WD=Q1039675|lt=Takamatsuzuka Kofun}}. * 1974: The Archaeological Museum of Kashihara became an affiliated facility of the institute. * 1985: First excavation of the Fujinoki Kofun. * 1990: Completion tier 6]], {{ill|Cultural properties of Japan|ja|埋蔵文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cultural properties of Japan|1=buried cultural property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11425912}} | Location = {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|en|Kashihara, Nara|WD=Q496319|lt=Kashihara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}, Japan | Website = [http://www.kashikoken.jp/museum/ Museum Official Site] }} The museum showcases approximately 3,700 artifacts from the Paleolithic through the Muromachi period.{{Cite web|和書|title=奈良の橿考研博物館リニューアル|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211102110118/https://nordot.app/828174813890314240|website=共同通信|accessdate=2021-11-03|language=ja}} Closed from December 24, 2018, for HVAC system updates, it reopened on November 3, 2021.[https://www.sankei.com/west/news/180417/wst1804170015-n1.html Museum closure announcement] {{lang|ja|産経ニュース}}, April 17, 2018. === Utilization Information === * Hours: 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM (last admission at 4:30 PM) * Closed: Mondays (or the following day if a holiday falls on a Monday), and from December 28 to January 4. * Admission Fees: ** Adults: ¥400 (¥350 for groups) ** Students (high school/university): ¥300 (¥250 for groups) ** Children (elementary/middle school): ¥200 (¥150 for groups) International tourists are admitted free of charge under Nara Prefecture's guidelines.{{Cite news|title=Why foreign tourists are free at Nara's museums while Japanese visitors pay?|newspaper=FNN|date=2024-03-07|url=https://www.fnn.jp/articles/-/667893|accessdate=2024-03-07}} === Highlights of Cultural Properties === * National Treasures and Important Cultural Properties include artifacts from significant kofun and archaeological sites in Nara Prefecture. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of personnel at the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所の人物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of personnel at the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == External Links == * {{official|http://www.kashikoken.jp/}} * {{Facebook|kashikoken|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute}} {{coord|region:JP|display=title}} {{translated page|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|version=104254781|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[file:Kashihara Archaeological Institute , Nara prefecture.jpg|thumb|200px|right|The Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute]] [[de:Arch%C3%A4ologisches Forschungsinstitut der Pr%C3%A4fektur Nara in Kashihara]] [[ja:奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]] {{Commons category|Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Forschungsinstitut der Präfektur Nara in Kashihara]] {{wikidata link|Q11445218}} [[Category: of the main building and other new facilities. * 1997: Excavation of the Kurozuka Kofun. * 2014: Establishment of the Uda Cultural Heritage Center at the site of the former Murō High School. * 2015: Transition from the Nara Prefectural Board of Education to the jurisdiction of the Governor of Nara Prefecture. == Organization == The institute is headed by a director, with departments including: * Deputy Director overseeing the Planning Division, Research Division, and General Affairs Section. * Attached Museum led by a museum director and a vice director, supervising the Curatorial Section. == Attached Museum == {{Museum | Name = Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Museum | revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture]][[de:Archäologisches Forschungsinstitut der Präfektur Nara in Kashihara]][[ja:奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]] [[co:commons:Category:Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture]][[de:Archäologisches Forschungsinstitut der Präfektur Nara in Kashihara]][[ja:奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]] [[co:commons:Category:Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture]][[de:Archäologisches Forschungsinstitut der Präfektur Nara in Kashihara]][[ja:奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]] [[sn:commons:Category:Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture]][[de:Archäologisches Forschungsinstitut der Präfektur Nara in Kashihara]][[ja:奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]] {{pp-vandalism|small=yes}} The '''Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute''' (奈良県立橿原考古学研究所, *Nara Kenritsu Kashihara Kōkogaku Kenkyūjo*) is a '''prefectural institution''' located in {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|en|Kashihara, Nara|WD=Q496319|lt=Kashihara, Nara}}, specializing in the research and investigation of '''buried cultural properties''' (埋蔵文化財). It is an institution under the jurisdiction of '''Nara Prefecture'''. As one of the oldest '''public institutions for archaeological research''' still in operation today, it is sometimes abbreviated as '''Kashikōken''' (橿考研). The institute has conducted '''excavations of major historical sites''' within Nara Prefecture, including the '''Makimuku Ruins''' ({{ill|Makimuku ruins|en|Makimuku ruins|WD=Q11608507|lt=Makimuku ruins}}), {{ill|Hokenoyama Kofun|en|Hokenoyama Kofun|WD=Q11337664|lt=Hokenoyama Kofun}}, {{ill|Kurozuka Kofun|en|Kurozuka Kofun|WD=Q1793171|lt=Kurozuka Kofun}}, {{ill|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun|en|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun|WD=Q11537882|lt=Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun}}, {{ill|Mesuriyama Kofun|en|Mesuriyama Kofun|WD=Q11343632|lt=Mesuriyama Kofun}}, {{ill|Fujinoki Tomb|en|Fujinoki Tomb|WD=Q3198273|lt=Fujinoki Kofun}}, and {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Tomb|en|Takamatsuzuka Tomb|WD=Q1039675|lt=Takamatsuzuka Kofun}}. It has gained '''national recognition''' for its work on '''excavations of ancient capitals''' such as '''Asuka-kyō''' ({{ill|Asuka-dera|ja|飛鳥京跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka-dera|1=Asuka-kyō Ruins|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665955}}), '''Tōdai-ji''' ({{ill|Tōdai-ji|en|Tōdai-ji|WD=Q460367|lt=Tōdai-ji}}), and '''Tōshōdai-ji''' ({{ill|Tōshōdai-ji|en|Tōshōdai-ji|WD=Q973175|lt=Tōshōdai-ji}}). The institute also manages an '''affiliated museum''' and operates a '''cultural property storage and research center''' in {{ill|Uda, Nara|en|Uda, Nara|WD=Q863747|lt=Uda, Nara}}. == History == * '''1938 (Shōwa 13)''' - Archaeological surveys of '''Kashihara Shrine’s outer precincts''' were conducted as part of the '''2600th Anniversary of Japan’s Founding Project''' (紀元二千六百年記念行事). '''Masao Suenaga''' ({{ill|Masao Suenaga|ja|末永雅雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masao Suenaga|1=Masao Suenaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11519783}}) from '''Kyoto University''' led the survey on '''September 13''', which later became the institute’s '''founding date'''. * '''1951 (Shōwa 26)''' - The '''Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute''' was officially established with '''Masao Suenaga''' as the first director. * '''1972 (Shōwa 47)''' - The '''Takamatsuzuka Kofun murals''' were discovered during an excavation by the institute. * '''1974 (Shōwa 49)''' - The former '''Kashihara Kōen Archaeological Museum''' became an '''affiliated facility''', later renamed '''Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum'''. * '''1985 (Shōwa 60)''' - First excavation of '''Fujinoki Kofun'''. * '''1990 (Heisei 2)''' - The institute’s '''new headquarters''' was completed. * '''1997 (Heisei 9)''' - Excavation of '''Kurozuka Kofun'''. * '''2014 (Heisei 26)''' * '''Cultural Property Storage and Research Center''' established in the former '''Nara Prefectural Murō High School'''. * '''2015 (Heisei 27)''' - The institute was transferred from the '''Nara Prefectural Board of Education''' to the '''Governor’s Office of Nara Prefecture'''. == Organization == * The '''Director-General''' oversees the '''Deputy Director''' and '''Museum Director'''. * The '''Deputy Director''' supervises: ** '''Planning Division''' (企画部) ** '''Planning Section''' (企画課) ** '''Documentation Section''' (資料課) ** '''Excavation Division''' (調査部) ** '''Excavation Section''' (調査課) ** '''General Affairs Section''' (総務課) * The '''Museum Director''' supervises the '''Deputy Museum Director''' and the '''Curatorial Section''' (学芸課). == Affiliated Museum == {{博物館 |名称 = Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum |image = Kashiharakouen-koukogaku02.jpg|Affiliated Museum |imagesize = 250 |正式名称 = 奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館 |専門分野 = {{ill|Archaeology|en|Archaeology|WD=Q23498|lt=Archaeology}}・{{ill|Buried Cultural Properties|ja|埋蔵文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buried Cultural Properties|1=buried cultural property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11425912}} |所在地 = [[Nara Prefecture]] [[Kashihara, Nara]] |アクセス = [[Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu]] {{ill|Kashihara Line|en|Kashihara Line|WD=Q2491066|lt=Kintetsu Kashihara Line}}, {{ill|Minami Osaka Line|en|Minami Osaka Line|WD=Q901342|lt=Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line}}, {{ill|Yoshino Line|en|Yoshino Line|WD=Q901085|lt=Kintetsu Yoshino Line}} - [[Kashiharajingū-mae Station]] (15-min walk)
[[Kintetsu Kashihara Line]] - [[Unebigoryōmae Station]] (5-min walk) |URL = [http://www.kashikoken.jp/museum/ Official Website] }} The '''museum houses approximately 3,700 artifacts''' spanning from the '''Paleolithic period to the Muromachi period'''.{{Cite web|和書|title=Nara's Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum Reopens|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211102110118/https://nordot.app/828174813890314240|website=Kyodo News|accessdate=2021-11-03|language=ja}} The museum was '''closed for renovations''' from '''December 24, 2018''', due to updates to the '''air conditioning system''' and concerns over the presence of '''harmful substances inside display cases'''. It '''reopened on November 3, 2021'''. == See Also == {{Commonscat|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture}} * == External Links == * {{official|http://www.kashikoken.jp/}} * {{Facebook|kashikoken|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute}} {{coord|region:JP|display=title}} {{translated page|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|version=104254781|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[file:Kashihara Archaeological Institute , Nara prefecture.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute]] [[de:Arch%C3%A4ologisches Forschungsinstitut der Pr%C3%A4fektur Nara in Kashihara]] ==Merged second translation== {{pp-vandalism|small=yes}} The '''Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute''' (奈良県立橿原考古学研究所, ''Nara Kenritsu Kashihara Kōkogaku Kenkyūsho'') is a prefectural research organization for archaeological excavations and studies, located in {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|en|Kashihara, Nara|WD=Q496319|lt=Kashihara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}, Japan. It is one of the oldest publicly operated archaeological institutes still in existence and is often abbreviated as '''Kashikōken''' (橿考研). The institute is renowned for its excavations of significant sites such as {{ill|Makimuku ruins|en|Makimuku ruins|WD=Q11608507|lt=Makimuku Ruins}}, {{ill|Hokenoyama Kofun|en|Hokenoyama Kofun|WD=Q11337664|lt=Hokenoyama Kofun}}, {{ill|Kurozuka Kofun|en|Kurozuka Kofun|WD=Q1793171|lt=Kurozuka Kofun}}, and {{ill|Fujinoki Tomb|en|Fujinoki Tomb|WD=Q3198273|lt=Fujinoki Tomb}}, as well as historical locations like {{ill|Asuka Palace Ruins|ja|飛鳥京跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka Palace Ruins|1=Asuka-kyō Ruins|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665955}}, {{ill|Tōdai-ji|en|Tōdai-ji|WD=Q460367|lt=Tōdai-ji}}, and {{ill|Tōshōdai-ji|en|Tōshōdai-ji|WD=Q973175|lt=Tōshōdai-ji}}. It also includes an attached museum and a facility in {{ill|Uda, Nara|en|Uda, Nara|WD=Q863747|lt=Uda}} for the preservation and organization of cultural artifacts. == History == * 1938: The institute's origins trace back to the "2600th Anniversary of the Imperial Era" commemorative events, during which {{ill|Kashihara Shrine|en|Kashihara Shrine|WD=Q710333|lt=Kashihara Shrine}} conducted an excavation of the {{ill|Kashihara Ruins|ja|橿原遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashihara Ruins|1=Kashihara Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24876647}} led by Masao Suenaga from Kyoto University. September 13, the start date of this excavation, is celebrated as the institute's founding date. * 1951: Officially established as the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute, with Masao Suenaga as its director. * 1972: Discovery of the mural paintings in {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Tomb|en|Takamatsuzuka Tomb|WD=Q1039675|lt=Takamatsuzuka Kofun}}. * 1974: The Archaeological Museum of Kashihara became an affiliated facility of the institute. * 1985: First excavation of the Fujinoki Kofun. * 1990: Completion tier 6]], {{ill|Cultural properties of Japan|ja|埋蔵文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cultural properties of Japan|1=buried cultural property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11425912}} | Location = {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|en|Kashihara, Nara|WD=Q496319|lt=Kashihara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}, Japan | Website = [http://www.kashikoken.jp/museum/ Museum Official Site] }} The museum showcases approximately 3,700 artifacts from the Paleolithic through the Muromachi period.{{Cite web|和書|title=奈良の橿考研博物館リニューアル|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211102110118/https://nordot.app/828174813890314240|website=共同通信|accessdate=2021-11-03|language=ja}} Closed from December 24, 2018, for HVAC system updates, it reopened on November 3, 2021.[https://www.sankei.com/west/news/180417/wst1804170015-n1.html Museum closure announcement] {{lang|ja|産経ニュース}}, April 17, 2018. === Utilization Information === * Hours: 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM (last admission at 4:30 PM) * Closed: Mondays (or the following day if a holiday falls on a Monday), and from December 28 to January 4. * Admission Fees: ** Adults: ¥400 (¥350 for groups) ** Students (high school/university): ¥300 (¥250 for groups) ** Children (elementary/middle school): ¥200 (¥150 for groups) International tourists are admitted free of charge under Nara Prefecture's guidelines.{{Cite news|title=Why foreign tourists are free at Nara's museums while Japanese visitors pay?|newspaper=FNN|date=2024-03-07|url=https://www.fnn.jp/articles/-/667893|accessdate=2024-03-07}} === Highlights of Cultural Properties === * National Treasures and Important Cultural Properties include artifacts from significant kofun and archaeological sites in Nara Prefecture. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of personnel at the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所の人物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of personnel at the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == External Links == * [[co:commons:Category:Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture]][[de:Archäologisches Forschungsinstitut der Präfektur Nara in Kashihara]][[ja:奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]] [[co:commons:Category:Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture]][[de:Archäologisches Forschungsinstitut der Präfektur Nara in Kashihara]][[ja:奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]] [[co:commons:Category:Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture]][[de:Archäologisches Forschungsinstitut der Präfektur Nara in Kashihara]][[ja:奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]] [[sn:commons:Category:Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture]][[de:Archäologisches Forschungsinstitut der Präfektur Nara in Kashihara]][[ja:奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]] {{pp-vandalism|small=yes}} The '''Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute''' (奈良県立橿原考古学研究所, *Nara Kenritsu Kashihara Kōkogaku Kenkyūjo*) is a '''prefectural institution''' located in {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|en|Kashihara, Nara|WD=Q496319|lt=Kashihara, Nara}}, specializing in the research and investigation of '''buried cultural properties''' (埋蔵文化財). It is an institution under the jurisdiction of '''Nara Prefecture'''. The institute has conducted '''excavations of major historical sites''' within Nara Prefecture, including the '''Makimuku Ruins''' ({{ill|Makimuku ruins|en|Makimuku ruins|WD=Q11608507|lt=Makimuku ruins}}), {{ill|Hokenoyama Kofun|en|Hokenoyama Kofun|WD=Q11337664|lt=Hokenoyama Kofun}}, {{ill|Kurozuka Kofun|en|Kurozuka Kofun|WD=Q1793171|lt=Kurozuka Kofun}}, {{ill|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun|en|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun|WD=Q11537882|lt=Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun}}, {{ill|Mesuriyama Kofun|en|Mesuriyama Kofun|WD=Q11343632|lt=Mesuriyama Kofun}}, {{ill|Fujinoki Tomb|en|Fujinoki Tomb|WD=Q3198273|lt=Fujinoki Kofun}}, and {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Tomb|en|Takamatsuzuka Tomb|WD=Q1039675|lt=Takamatsuzuka Kofun}}. It has gained '''national recognition''' for its work on '''excavations of ancient capitals''' such as '''Asuka-kyō''' ({{ill|Asuka-dera|ja|飛鳥京跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka-dera|1=Asuka-kyō Ruins|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665955}}), '''Tōdai-ji''' ({{ill|Tōdai-ji|en|Tōdai-ji|WD=Q460367|lt=Tōdai-ji}}), and '''Tōshōdai-ji''' ({{ill|Tōshōdai-ji|en|Tōshōdai-ji|WD=Q973175|lt=Tōshōdai-ji}}). The institute also manages an '''affiliated museum''' and operates a '''cultural property storage and research center''' in {{ill|Uda, Nara|en|Uda, Nara|WD=Q863747|lt=Uda, Nara}}. == History == * '''1938 (Shōwa 13)''' - Archaeological surveys of '''Kashihara Shrine’s outer precincts''' were conducted as part of the '''2600th Anniversary of Japan’s Founding Project''' (紀元二千六百年記念行事). '''Masao Suenaga''' ({{ill|Masao Suenaga|ja|末永雅雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masao Suenaga|1=Masao Suenaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11519783}}) from '''Kyoto University''' led the survey on '''September 13''', which later became the institute’s '''founding date'''. * '''1951 (Shōwa 26)''' - The '''Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute''' was officially established with '''Masao Suenaga''' as the first director. * '''1972 (Shōwa 47)''' - The '''Takamatsuzuka Kofun murals''' were discovered during an excavation by the institute. * '''1974 (Shōwa 49)''' - The former '''Kashihara Kōen Archaeological Museum''' became an '''affiliated facility''', later renamed '''Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum'''. * '''1985 (Shōwa 60)''' - First excavation of '''Fujinoki Kofun'''. * '''1990 (Heisei 2)''' - The institute’s '''new headquarters''' was completed. * '''1997 (Heisei 9)''' - Excavation of '''Kurozuka Kofun'''. * '''2014 (Heisei 26)''' * '''Cultural Property Storage and Research Center''' established in the former '''Nara Prefectural Murō High School'''. * '''2015 (Heisei 27)''' - The institute was transferred from the '''Nara Prefectural Board of Education''' to the '''Governor’s Office of Nara Prefecture'''. == Organization == * The '''Director-General''' oversees the '''Deputy Director''' and '''Museum Director'''. * The '''Deputy Director''' supervises: ** '''Planning Division''' (企画部) ** '''Planning Section''' (企画課) ** '''Documentation Section''' (資料課) ** '''Excavation Division''' (調査部) ** '''Excavation Section''' (調査課) ** '''General Affairs Section''' (総務課) * The '''Museum Director''' supervises the '''Deputy Museum Director''' and the '''Curatorial Section''' (学芸課). == Affiliated Museum == {{博物館 |名称 = Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum |image = Kashiharakouen-koukogaku02.jpg|Affiliated Museum |imagesize = 250 |正式名称 = 奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館 |専門分野 = {{ill|Archaeology|en|Archaeology|WD=Q23498|lt=Archaeology}}・{{ill|Buried Cultural Properties|ja|埋蔵文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buried Cultural Properties|1=buried cultural property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11425912}} |所在地 = [[Nara Prefecture]] [[Kashihara, Nara]] |アクセス = [[Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu]] {{ill|Kashihara Line|en|Kashihara Line|WD=Q2491066|lt=Kintetsu Kashihara Line}}, {{ill|Minami Osaka Line|en|Minami Osaka Line|WD=Q901342|lt=Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line}}, {{ill|Yoshino Line|en|Yoshino Line|WD=Q901085|lt=Kintetsu Yoshino Line}} - [[Kashiharajingū-mae Station]] (15-min walk)
[[Kintetsu Kashihara Line]] - [[Unebigoryōmae Station]] (5-min walk) |URL = [http://www.kashikoken.jp/museum/ Official Website] }} The '''museum houses approximately 3,700 artifacts''' spanning from the '''Paleolithic period to the Muromachi period'''.{{Cite web|和書|title=Nara's Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum Reopens|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211102110118/https://nordot.app/828174813890314240|website=Kyodo News|accessdate=2021-11-03|language=ja}} The museum was '''closed for renovations''' from '''December 24, 2018''', due to updates to the '''air conditioning system''' and concerns over the presence of '''harmful substances inside display cases'''. It '''reopened on November 3, 2021'''. == See Also == {{Commonscat|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture}} * == External Links == * {{official|http://www.kashikoken.jp/}} * {{Facebook|kashikoken|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute}} {{coord|region:JP|display=title}} {{translated page|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|version=104254781|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[file:Kashihara Archaeological Institute , Nara prefecture.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute]] [[de:Arch%C3%A4ologisches Forschungsinstitut der Pr%C3%A4fektur Nara in Kashihara]] ==Merged second translation== {{pp-vandalism|small=yes}} The '''Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute''' (奈良県立橿原考古学研究所, ''Nara Kenritsu Kashihara Kōkogaku Kenkyūsho'') is a prefectural research organization for archaeological excavations and studies, located in {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|en|Kashihara, Nara|WD=Q496319|lt=Kashihara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}, Japan. It is one of the oldest publicly operated archaeological institutes still in existence and is often abbreviated as '''Kashikōken''' (橿考研). The institute is renowned for its excavations of significant sites such as {{ill|Makimuku ruins|en|Makimuku ruins|WD=Q11608507|lt=Makimuku Ruins}}, {{ill|Hokenoyama Kofun|en|Hokenoyama Kofun|WD=Q11337664|lt=Hokenoyama Kofun}}, {{ill|Kurozuka Kofun|en|Kurozuka Kofun|WD=Q1793171|lt=Kurozuka Kofun}}, and {{ill|Fujinoki Tomb|en|Fujinoki Tomb|WD=Q3198273|lt=Fujinoki Tomb}}, as well as historical locations like {{ill|Asuka Palace Ruins|ja|飛鳥京跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka Palace Ruins|1=Asuka-kyō Ruins|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665955}}, {{ill|Tōdai-ji|en|Tōdai-ji|WD=Q460367|lt=Tōdai-ji}}, and {{ill|Tōshōdai-ji|en|Tōshōdai-ji|WD=Q973175|lt=Tōshōdai-ji}}. It also includes an attached museum and a facility in {{ill|Uda, Nara|en|Uda, Nara|WD=Q863747|lt=Uda}} for the preservation and organization of cultural artifacts. == History == * 1938: The institute's origins trace back to the "2600th Anniversary of the Imperial Era" commemorative events, during which {{ill|Kashihara Shrine|en|Kashihara Shrine|WD=Q710333|lt=Kashihara Shrine}} conducted an excavation of the {{ill|Kashihara Ruins|ja|橿原遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashihara Ruins|1=Kashihara Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24876647}} led by Masao Suenaga from Kyoto University. September 13, the start date of this excavation, is celebrated as the institute's founding date. * 1951: Officially established as the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute, with Masao Suenaga as its director. * 1972: Discovery of the mural paintings in {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Tomb|en|Takamatsuzuka Tomb|WD=Q1039675|lt=Takamatsuzuka Kofun}}. * 1974: The Archaeological Museum of Kashihara became an affiliated facility of the institute. * 1985: First excavation of the Fujinoki Kofun. * 1990: Completion tier 6]], {{ill|Cultural properties of Japan|ja|埋蔵文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cultural properties of Japan|1=buried cultural property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11425912}} | Location = {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|en|Kashihara, Nara|WD=Q496319|lt=Kashihara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}, Japan | Website = [http://www.kashikoken.jp/museum/ Museum Official Site] }} The museum showcases approximately 3,700 artifacts from the Paleolithic through the Muromachi period.{{Cite web|和書|title=奈良の橿考研博物館リニューアル|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211102110118/https://nordot.app/828174813890314240|website=共同通信|accessdate=2021-11-03|language=ja}} Closed from December 24, 2018, for HVAC system updates, it reopened on November 3, 2021.[https://www.sankei.com/west/news/180417/wst1804170015-n1.html Museum closure announcement] {{lang|ja|産経ニュース}}, April 17, 2018. === Utilization Information === * Hours: 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM (last admission at 4:30 PM) * Closed: Mondays (or the following day if a holiday falls on a Monday), and from December 28 to January 4. * Admission Fees: ** Adults: ¥400 (¥350 for groups) ** Students (high school/university): ¥300 (¥250 for groups) ** Children (elementary/middle school): ¥200 (¥150 for groups) International tourists are admitted free of charge under Nara Prefecture's guidelines.{{Cite news|title=Why foreign tourists are free at Nara's museums while Japanese visitors pay?|newspaper=FNN|date=2024-03-07|url=https://www.fnn.jp/articles/-/667893|accessdate=2024-03-07}} === Highlights of Cultural Properties === * National Treasures and Important Cultural Properties include artifacts from significant kofun and archaeological sites in Nara Prefecture. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of personnel at the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所の人物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of personnel at the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == External Links == * {{official|http://www.kashikoken.jp/}} * {{Facebook|kashikoken|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute}} {{coord|region:JP|display=title}} {{translated page|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|version=104254781|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[file:Kashihara Archaeological Institute , Nara prefecture.jpg|thumb|200px|right|The Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute]] [[de:Arch%C3%A4ologisches Forschungsinstitut der Pr%C3%A4fektur Nara in Kashihara]] [[ja:奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]] {{Commons category|Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture}} [[de:Archäologisches Forschungsinstitut der Präfektur Nara in Kashihara]] {{Commons category|Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture}} [[de:Archäologisches Forschungsinstitut der Präfektur Nara in Kashihara]] {{wikidata link|Q11445218}} [[Category: of the main building and other new facilities. * 1997: Excavation of the Kurozuka Kofun. * 2014: Establishment of the Uda Cultural Heritage Center at the site of the former Murō High School. * 2015: Transition from the Nara Prefectural Board of Education to the jurisdiction of the Governor of Nara Prefecture. == Organization == The institute is headed by a director, with departments including: * Deputy Director overseeing the Planning Division, Research Division, and General Affairs Section. * Attached Museum led by a museum director and a vice director, supervising the Curatorial Section. == Attached Museum == {{Museum | Name = Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Museum |
